structures.desc: Regular plural endings (-s, -es, -ies) and common irregular plurals.

rules.t: Most nouns form the plural by adding -s.
rules.td: Die meisten Nomen bilden die Mehrzahl mit -s.

rules.t: Nouns ending in -s, -ss, -sh, -ch, -x, -o add -es.
rules.td: Nomen, die auf -s, -ss, -sh, -ch, -x oder -o enden, bekommen -es.

rules.t: Nouns ending in consonant + -y change -y to -ies.
rules.td: Nomen, die auf Konsonant + -y enden, werden zu -ies.

rules.t: Some nouns have irregular plural forms that must be learnt by heart.
rules.td: Manche Nomen haben unregelmäßige Mehrzahlformen, die man auswendig lernen muss.

affirmative: There are three children in the park.

errs.w: There are three childs in the garden.
errs.c: There are three children in the garden.

structures.desc: Positive and negative commands using the base form of the verb.

rules.t: Use the base form of the verb (without 'to') to give a command.
rules.td: Verwende die Grundform des Verbs (ohne 'to') für einen Befehl.

rules.t: Use 'Don't' + base form for negative commands.
rules.td: Verwende 'Don't' + Grundform für negative Befehle.

errs.d: Including the subject 'you' in the imperative
Using 'not' instead of 'don't' for negative imperatives

structures.desc: Common contractions used in spoken and informal written English: I'm, What's, it's, isn't, Let's.

rules.t: Contractions shorten two words into one using an apostrophe (') where letters are missing.
rules.td: Kurzformen verkürzen zwei Wörter zu einem Wort. Der Apostroph (') zeigt, wo Buchstaben fehlen.

rules.t: Don't confuse 'it's' (= it is) with 'its' (= possessive, belonging to it).
rules.td: Verwechsle nicht 'it's' (= it is) mit 'its' (= sein/ihr, Besitz).

ex.en: It's a nice day. (= It is)
The dog loves its ball. (= possession)

errs.w: The cat is eating it's food.
errs.c: The cat is eating its food.

structures.desc: The verb 'to be' in affirmative, negative and question forms: am/is/are.

rules.t: I am / you are / he-she-it is / we are / they are.
rules.td: I am / you are / he-she-it is / we are / they are.

rules.t: Negative: am not / isn't (is not) / aren't (are not). Note: 'I'm not' (no contraction 'amn't').
rules.td: Verneinung: am not / isn't (is not) / aren't (are not). Beachte: 'I'm not' (keine Kurzform 'amn't').

rules.t: Questions: swap subject and 'be'. Am I? / Is he? / Are they? Short answers: Yes, I am. / No, he isn't.
rules.td: Fragen: Tausche Subjekt und 'be'. Am I? / Is he? / Are they? Kurzantworten: Yes, I am. / No, he isn't.

ex.en: Are you happy? – Yes, I am.
ex.de: Bist du glücklich? – Ja, bin ich.

ex.en: Is she your sister? – No, she isn't.
ex.de: Ist sie deine Schwester? – Nein, ist sie nicht.

errs.d: Using the wrong form of 'be' for the subject
Using a contraction in affirmative short answers

structures.desc: Basic prepositions showing where something is: in, on, under, behind, next to, in front of.

rules.t: Use 'in' (inside), 'on' (on a surface), 'under' (below), 'behind' (at the back), 'next to' (beside), 'in front of' (facing).
rules.td: Verwende 'in' (drinnen), 'on' (auf einer Oberflaeche), 'under' (darunter), 'behind' (dahinter), 'next to' (daneben), 'in front of' (davor).

ex.en: The book is on the table.
ex.de: Das Buch ist auf dem Tisch.

ex.en: The cat is under the bed.
ex.de: Die Katze ist unter dem Bett.

ex.en: The school is next to the park.
ex.de: Die Schule ist neben dem Park.

ex.en: The car is in front of the house.
ex.de: Das Auto ist vor dem Haus.

affirmative: The pen is in my bag.
The picture is on the wall.
negative: The keys aren't on the table.

errs.w: The tree is in front the house.
errs.c: The tree is in front of the house.

structures.nd: there is / there are – es gibt
structures.desc: Using 'there is' for singular and 'there are' for plural to say that something exists.

rules.t: Use 'there is' (there's) for singular or uncountable nouns.
rules.td: Verwende 'there is' (there's) für Einzahl oder unzaehlbare Nomen.

ex.en: There is a park near my house.
ex.de: Es gibt einen Park in der Nähe meines Hauses.

ex.en: There's a cat on the roof.
ex.de: Es gibt eine Katze auf dem Dach.

rules.t: Use 'there are' for plural nouns.
rules.td: Verwende 'there are' für Mehrzahl-Nomen.

ex.en: There are three bedrooms in my flat.
ex.de: Es gibt drei Schlafzimmer in meiner Wohnung.

ex.en: There are many students in our class.
ex.de: Es gibt viele Schueler in unserer Klasse.

affirmative: There is a museum in our town.
There are twenty students in our class.

errs.w: There is three monkeys in the zoo.
errs.c: There are three monkeys in the zoo.

errs.w: It has a park in our town.
errs.c: There is a park in our town.

structures.desc: The personal pronouns used as the subject of a sentence: I, you, he, she, it, we, they.

rules.t: Subject pronouns replace a noun as the subject: I, you, he, she, it, we, they.
rules.td: Subjektpronomen ersetzen ein Nomen als Subjekt: I, you, he, she, it, we, they.

ex.en: Tom is my friend. He is twelve.
ex.de: Tom ist mein Freund. Er ist zwoelf.

ex.en: The book is new. It is interesting.
ex.de: Das Buch ist neu. Es ist interessant.

rules.t: Use 'it' for animals (when gender is unknown) and things, never for people.
rules.td: Verwende 'it' für Tiere (wenn das Geschlecht unbekannt ist) und Dinge, nie für Menschen.

ex.en: Where is the dog? It is in the garden.
ex.de: Wo ist der Hund? Er ist im Garten.

ex.en: This is my sister. She is nice.
ex.de: Das ist meine Schwester. Sie ist nett.

errs.w: The table is big. He is brown.
errs.c: The table is big. It is brown.

errs.w: This is my teacher. It is very nice.
errs.c: This is my teacher. She is very nice.

structures.desc: Expressing possession with 'have got' in affirmative, negative and question forms.

rules.t: I/you/we/they have got … He/she/it has got …
rules.td: I/you/we/they have got … He/she/it has got …

rules.t: Negative: haven't got / hasn't got.
rules.td: Verneinung: haven't got / hasn't got.

rules.t: Questions: Have you got …? / Has she got …? Short answers: Yes, I have. / No, she hasn't.
rules.td: Fragen: Have you got …? / Has she got …? Kurzantworten: Yes, I have. / No, she hasn't.

ex.en: Have you got a bike? – Yes, I have.
ex.de: Hast du ein Fahrrad? – Ja, habe ich.

ex.en: Has he got a dog? – No, he hasn't.
ex.de: Hat er einen Hund? – Nein, hat er nicht.

errs.w: He have got a new bike.
errs.c: He has got a new bike.

errs.w: Does he has got a cat?

structures.desc: Showing possession by adding 's to a name or noun: Tom's book, my sister's room.

rules.t: Add 's to the owner's name or noun: Name + 's + thing owned.
rules.td: Hänge 's an den Namen oder das Nomen des Besitzers: Name + 's + Besitz.

ex.de: Der Name meiner Schwester ist Anna.

rules.t: Don't confuse possessive 's with the 'is' contraction. Context helps you decide.
rules.td: Verwechsle das Besitz-'s nicht mit der Kurzform von 'is'. Der Kontext hilft dir.

ex.en: Tom's book is new. (= the book belonging to Tom)

affirmative: My mum's birthday is in May.

errs.w: Tom's is nice. (meaning: Tom's bag is nice)

structures.desc: Using adjectives after the verb 'be': Subject + be + adjective.

rules.t: Adjectives come after the verb 'be': Subject + am/is/are + adjective.
rules.td: Adjektive stehen nach dem Verb 'be': Subjekt + am/is/are + Adjektiv.

rules.t: English adjectives do not change for singular/plural or gender.
rules.td: Englische Adjektive verändern sich nicht für Einzahl/Mehrzahl oder Geschlecht.

ex.en: The boy is tall. The girls are tall.
ex.de: Der Bub ist gross. Die Mädchen sind gross.

errs.d: Placing the adverb 'very' before 'be' instead of before the adjective
Omitting the verb 'be' before an adjective

structures.desc: Using 'because' to give a reason: statement + because + reason (SVO word order).

rules.t: Use 'because' to give a reason. The word order after 'because' is normal SVO (subject + verb + object).
rules.td: Verwende 'because' für eine Begründung. Die Wortstellung nach 'because' ist normal SVO (Subjekt + Verb + Objekt).

ex.en: I like summer because it is warm.
ex.de: Ich mag den Sommer, weil es warm ist.

ex.en: She is happy because she has a new bike.
ex.de: Sie ist glücklich, weil sie ein neues Fahrrad hat.

affirmative: I'm tired because I got up early.
He likes dogs because they are friendly.
negative: I don't like winter because it is cold.

errs.d: Using German V-final word order after 'because'
errs.w: I like summer because warm it is.
errs.c: I like summer because it is warm.

errs.w: I'm happy because of I have a dog.
errs.c: I'm happy because I have a dog.

structures.desc: The seven days of the week, always capitalised in English, used with the preposition 'on'.

rules.t: Days of the week always start with a capital letter in English: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday.
rules.td: Wochentage werden im Englischen immer grossgeschrieben: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday.

rules.t: Use the preposition 'on' with days: on Monday, on Fridays.
rules.td: Verwende die Präposition 'on' mit Wochentagen: on Monday, on Fridays.

negative: We don't have school on Sunday.
questions: Do you have music on Thursday?

errs.d: Not capitalising days of the week
Using 'at' or 'in' instead of 'on' with days

structures.desc: Words that show who something belongs to: my, your, his, her, its, our, their.

rules.t: Possessive adjectives go before a noun: my, your, his, her, its, our, their.
rules.td: Possessivbegleiter stehen vor dem Nomen: my, your, his, her, its, our, their.

rules.t: 'its' (no apostrophe) = possessive; 'it's' (with apostrophe) = it is.
rules.td: 'its' (ohne Apostroph) = Besitz; 'it's' (mit Apostroph) = it is.

ex.de: Der Hund wedelt mit seinem Schwanz.

affirmative: His sister is in our class.

errs.d: Using subject pronouns instead of possessive adjectives

structures.desc: Expressing ability and permission with 'can' and 'can't': can + base verb (no -s, no 'to').

rules.t: Subject + can + base verb (infinitive without 'to'). 'Can' is the same for all persons (no -s for he/she/it).
rules.td: Subjekt + can + Grundform (Infinitiv ohne 'to'). 'Can' ist für alle Personen gleich (kein -s bei he/she/it).

rules.t: Negative: can't (cannot) + base verb.
rules.td: Verneinung: can't (cannot) + Grundform.

rules.t: Questions: Can + subject + base verb? Short answers: Yes, I can. / No, I can't.
rules.td: Fragen: Can + Subjekt + Grundform? Kurzantworten: Yes, I can. / No, I can't.

ex.en: Can you speak English? – Yes, I can.
ex.de: Kannst du Englisch sprechen? – Ja, kann ich.

ex.en: Can he cook? – No, he can't.
ex.de: Kann er kochen? – Nein, kann er nicht.

structures.desc: Describing habits and facts using the present simple affirmative: I/you/we/they + base verb, he/she/it + verb-s.

rules.t: I/you/we/they use the base form of the verb.
rules.td: I/you/we/they verwenden die Grundform des Verbs.

rules.t: He/she/it adds -s (or -es/-ies) to the verb.
rules.td: He/she/it bekommt -s (oder -es/-ies) ans Verb.

rules.t: Spelling rules for 3rd person: verbs ending in -s, -sh, -ch, -x, -o add -es; verbs ending in consonant + -y change to -ies.
rules.td: Rechtschreibregeln für die 3. Person: Verben auf -s, -sh, -ch, -x, -o bekommen -es; Verben auf Konsonant + -y werden zu -ies.

ex.en: go → goes, wash → washes, try → tries
ex.de: go → goes, wash → washes, try → tries

affirmative: She goes to school by bus.

errs.d: Forgetting the -s for he/she/it (THE #1 learner error)
Using German V2 word order with time adverbs

structures.nd: a lot of / lots of – viel(e)
structures.desc: Using 'a lot of' and 'lots of' with countable and uncountable nouns to express a large quantity.

rules.t: 'A lot of' and 'lots of' mean 'many/much' and can be used with both countable and uncountable nouns.
rules.td: 'A lot of' und 'lots of' bedeuten 'viel/viele' und können mit zaehlbaren und unzaehlbaren Nomen verwendet werden.

ex.en: I have a lot of friends.

affirmative: She has a lot of homework.
There are a lot of students.
negative: I don't have a lot of time.
questions: Do you have a lot of friends?

errs.d: Writing 'a lots of' instead of 'a lot of'
errs.w: I have a lots of books.
errs.c: I have a lot of books.
She has a lot of friends.

structures.desc: Forming negative sentences in the present simple with don't / doesn't + base verb.

rules.t: I/you/we/they + don't (do not) + base verb.
rules.td: I/you/we/they + don't (do not) + Grundform.

rules.t: He/she/it + doesn't (does not) + base verb (no -s on the verb!).
rules.td: He/she/it + doesn't (does not) + Grundform (kein -s am Verb!).

errs.d: Keeping the -s on the verb after 'doesn't' (double marking)

structures.desc: The indefinite articles 'a' (before consonant sounds) and 'an' (before vowel sounds).

rules.t: Use 'a' before words that start with a consonant sound.
rules.td: Verwende 'a' vor Wörtern, die mit einem Konsonantenlaut beginnen.

ex.en: a book, a cat, a university
ex.de: ein Buch, eine Katze, eine Universitaet

rules.t: Use 'an' before words that start with a vowel sound.
rules.td: Verwende 'an' vor Wörtern, die mit einem Vokallaut beginnen.

ex.en: an apple, an elephant, an hour
ex.de: ein Apfel, ein Elefant, eine Stunde

questions: Is it a cat or a dog?

errs.w: I eat a apple every day.
errs.c: I eat an apple every day.

errs.d: Using 'an' before words starting with a consonant sound despite vowel spelling

structures.desc: Frequency adverbs (always, usually, often, sometimes, never) and their position in the sentence.

rules.t: Frequency adverbs come before the main verb: Subject + adverb + verb.
rules.td: Häufigkeitsadverbien stehen vor dem Hauptverb: Subjekt + Adverb + Verb.

rules.t: Frequency adverbs come after the verb 'be': Subject + be + adverb.
rules.td: Häufigkeitsadverbien stehen nach dem Verb 'be': Subjekt + be + Adverb.

affirmative: She usually gets up at seven.

errs.d: Placing the adverb after the main verb (German word order)
Placing the adverb before 'be' instead of after it

structures.desc: Forming yes/no questions and short answers in the present simple with Do/Does + subject + base verb.

rules.t: Do + I/you/we/they + base verb?
rules.td: Do + I/you/we/they + Grundform?

ex.en: Do you like pizza? – Yes, I do.
Do they play football? – No, they don't.

rules.t: Does + he/she/it + base verb? (no -s on the verb!)
rules.td: Does + he/she/it + Grundform? (kein -s am Verb!)

ex.en: Does she like music? – Yes, she does.
Does he play the piano? – No, he doesn't.

errs.d: Keeping the -s on the main verb in 'does' questions

structures.desc: Personal pronouns used as the object of a verb or after a preposition: me, you, him, her, it, us, them.

rules.t: Object pronouns replace a noun as the object: me, you, him, her, it, us, them.
rules.td: Objektpronomen ersetzen ein Nomen als Objekt: me, you, him, her, it, us, them.

ex.en: I like him. (not: I like he)

rules.t: After prepositions, always use object pronouns.
rules.td: Nach Präpositionen verwendet man immer Objektpronomen.

structures.desc: Using question words (who, what, where, how often, why) to ask open questions.

rules.t: Common question words: who (person), what (thing), where (place), why (reason), how often (frequency). Structure: Question word + do/does + subject + base verb?
rules.td: Wichtige Fragewörter: who (Person), what (Sache), where (Ort), why (Grund), how often (Häufigkeit). Struktur: Fragewort + do/does + Subjekt + Grundform?

ex.en: How often do you play football?

rules.t: When 'who' is the subject, don't use do/does: Who likes pizza? (not: Who does like pizza?)
rules.td: Wenn 'who' das Subjekt ist, kein do/does: Who likes pizza? (nicht: Who does like pizza?)

ex.en: Who speaks French in your class?
ex.de: Wer spricht Franzoesisch in deiner Klasse?

questions: What do you eat for breakfast?

structures.desc: Time expressions for frequency: once a week, twice a day, three times a month, every day.

rules.t: Use 'once' (= one time), 'twice' (= two times), 'three times', etc. + a day/week/month/year.
rules.td: Verwende 'once' (= einmal), 'twice' (= zweimal), 'three times' usw. + a day/week/month/year.

ex.en: I brush my teeth twice a day.
ex.de: Ich putze mir zweimal am Tag die Zähne.

ex.en: We have English three times a week.
ex.de: Wir haben dreimal pro Woche Englisch.

rules.t: 'Every' + singular noun: every day, every week, every Monday.
rules.td: 'Every' + Nomen in der Einzahl: every day, every week, every Monday.

affirmative: I play tennis once a week.
She goes swimming twice a week.
questions: How often do you go to the cinema?

errs.w: I go swimming one time a week.
errs.c: I go swimming once a week.

errs.w: I eat fruit two times a day.
errs.c: I eat fruit twice a day.

structures.desc: Pointing words: this/these (near) and that/those (far), matching singular and plural.

rules.t: Use 'this' (singular) and 'these' (plural) for things near you.
rules.td: Verwende 'this' (Einzahl) und 'these' (Mehrzahl) für Dinge in deiner Nähe.

rules.t: Use 'that' (singular) and 'those' (plural) for things far from you.
rules.td: Verwende 'that' (Einzahl) und 'those' (Mehrzahl) für Dinge, die weiter weg sind.

errs.d: Using singular demonstrative with plural noun

structures.nd: How much is / are …? – Wie viel kostet …?
structures.desc: Asking about prices with 'How much is …?' (singular) and 'How much are …?' (plural).

rules.t: How much is + singular noun? Use 'is' for one thing.
rules.td: How much is + Nomen (Einzahl)? Verwende 'is' für eine Sache.

ex.en: How much is this T-shirt? – It's fifteen euros.
ex.de: Wie viel kostet dieses T-Shirt? – Es kostet fünfzehn Euro.

rules.t: How much are + plural noun? Use 'are' for more than one thing.
rules.td: How much are + Nomen (Mehrzahl)? Verwende 'are' für mehrere Sachen.

ex.en: How much are the shoes? – They're forty euros.
ex.de: Wie viel kosten die Schuhe? – Sie kosten vierzig Euro.

structures.desc: Actions happening now: am/is/are + verb-ing in affirmative, negative and question forms.

rules.t: Subject + am/is/are + verb-ing. Used for actions happening right now.
rules.td: Subjekt + am/is/are + Verb-ing. Wird für Handlungen verwendet, die gerade jetzt passieren.

rules.t: Negative: Subject + am not / isn't / aren't + verb-ing.
rules.td: Verneinung: Subjekt + am not / isn't / aren't + Verb-ing.

rules.t: Questions: Am/Is/Are + subject + verb-ing? Short answers: Yes, I am. / No, she isn't.
rules.td: Fragen: Am/Is/Are + Subjekt + Verb-ing? Kurzantworten: Yes, I am. / No, she isn't.

ex.en: Are you listening? – Yes, I am.
Is he sleeping? – No, he isn't.

rules.t: Spelling rules: drop final -e before -ing (make → making); double the final consonant after a short vowel (sit → sitting, run → running).
rules.td: Rechtschreibregeln: -e am Ende faellt weg vor -ing (make → making); Verdopplung des Endkonsonanten nach kurzem Vokal (sit → sitting, run → running).

ex.en: write → writing, swim → swimming, dance → dancing
ex.de: write → writing, swim → swimming, dance → dancing

errs.c: She is playing football right now.

errs.d: Using do-support for present continuous questions
Forgetting to double the consonant before -ing
errs.w: He is siting on the chair.
errs.c: He is sitting on the chair.

structures.desc: The past tense of 'be': was (I/he/she/it), were (you/we/they) in all forms.

rules.t: I/he/she/it + was. You/we/they + were.
rules.td: I/he/she/it + was. You/we/they + were.

ex.en: They were in London last summer.
ex.de: Sie waren letzten Sommer in London.

rules.t: Negative: wasn't (was not), weren't (were not).
rules.td: Verneinung: wasn't (was not), weren't (were not).

ex.de: Wir waren nicht in der Schule.

rules.t: Questions: Was I/he/she/it …? Were you/we/they …? Short answers: Yes, I was. / No, they weren't.
rules.td: Fragen: Was I/he/she/it …? Were you/we/they …? Kurzantworten: Yes, I was. / No, they weren't.

ex.en: Was it cold? – Yes, it was.
ex.de: War es kalt? – Ja, war es.

ex.en: Were you at the party? – No, I wasn't.
ex.de: Warst du bei der Party? – Nein, war ich nicht.

errs.d: Using 'were' with I or 'was' with they

structures.desc: Ordinal numbers (1st–31st) with spelling rules: most add -th, with exceptions for 1st, 2nd, 3rd.

rules.t: Most ordinal numbers add -th to the cardinal number: four → fourth, six → sixth, seven → seventh.
rules.td: Die meisten Ordnungszahlen hängen -th an die Grundzahl: four → fourth, six → sixth, seven → seventh.

ex.en: She is in the fourth grade.
ex.de: Sie ist in der vierten Klasse.

ex.en: Today is the seventh of March.

rules.t: Exceptions: first (1st), second (2nd), third (3rd). Spelling changes: five → fifth, eight → eighth, nine → ninth, twelve → twelfth.
rules.td: Ausnahmen: first (1st), second (2nd), third (3rd). Rechtschreibaenderungen: five → fifth, eight → eighth, nine → ninth, twelve → twelfth.

ex.en: He came first in the race.
My birthday is on the twenty-third of May.
ex.de: Mein Geburtstag ist am dreiundzwanzigsten Mai.

affirmative: She lives on the third floor.
Today is the fifteenth of December.
questions: What's the date today? – It's the twenty-first of June.

errs.w: She was fiveth in the race.
errs.c: She was fifth in the race.

errs.w: His birthday is on the nineth.
errs.c: His birthday is on the ninth.

structures.desc: Prepositions used with time expressions: on (days/dates), in (months/seasons), at (times).

rules.t: Use 'on' for days and dates: on Monday, on the 5th of May, on my birthday.
rules.td: Verwende 'on' für Tage und Daten: on Monday, on the 5th of May, on my birthday.

ex.en: I have a test on Friday.
ex.de: Ich habe am Freitag einen Test.

ex.en: Her birthday is on the 3rd of April.

rules.t: Use 'in' for months, seasons, and longer periods: in January, in summer, in the morning.
rules.td: Verwende 'in' für Monate, Jahreszeiten und längere Zeitraeume: in January, in summer, in the morning.

ex.en: I wake up early in the morning.
ex.de: Ich wache frueh am Morgen auf.

rules.t: Use 'at' for clock times and fixed time points: at 7 o'clock, at night, at the weekend.
rules.td: Verwende 'at' für Uhrzeiten und feste Zeitpunkte: at 7 o'clock, at night, at the weekend.

affirmative: I go to bed at ten.
questions: When is the party? – On Saturday at seven.

structures.desc: Forming the past simple of regular verbs by adding -ed, with spelling rules for -d, -ied, and doubled consonants.

rules.t: Add -ed to most verbs: play → played, walk → walked, want → wanted.
rules.td: Hänge -ed an die meisten Verben: play → played, walk → walked, want → wanted.

ex.de: Sie ist zu Fuß in die Schule gegangen.

rules.t: Spelling rules: verbs ending in -e add -d (like → liked); consonant + y changes to -ied (study → studied); short vowel + consonant doubles the consonant (stop → stopped).
rules.td: Rechtschreibregeln: Verben auf -e bekommen nur -d (like → liked); Konsonant + y wird zu -ied (study → studied); kurzer Vokal + Konsonant verdoppelt den Konsonanten (stop → stopped).

ex.de: Er hat für den Test gelernt.

errs.w: The bus stoped at the corner.
errs.c: The bus stopped at the corner.

structures.desc: Basic linking words to join ideas: and (addition), but (contrast), because (reason).

rules.t: 'And' joins two similar ideas (addition).
rules.td: 'And' verbindet zwei aehnliche Ideen (Hinzufuegung).

rules.t: 'But' joins two contrasting ideas.
rules.td: 'But' verbindet zwei gegensätzliche Ideen.

ex.en: I like cats but I don't like dogs.
ex.de: Ich mag Katzen, aber ich mag keine Hunde.

ex.en: He is tall but he can't play basketball.
ex.de: Er ist gross, aber er kann nicht Basketball spielen.

rules.t: 'Because' gives a reason. Normal SVO word order follows.
rules.td: 'Because' gibt einen Grund an. Normale SVO-Wortstellung folgt.

ex.en: I stayed at home because I was ill.
ex.de: Ich blieb zu Hause, weil ich krank war.

affirmative: I played football and I watched TV.
She was tired but she didn't go to bed.
We stayed inside because it rained.

errs.w: I woke up and I had breakfast and I went to school and I played football.
errs.c: I woke up and had breakfast. Then I went to school. After school, I played football.

errs.d: Using German V-final word order after 'because'
errs.w: I was happy because a good mark I got.
errs.c: I was happy because I got a good mark.

structures.desc: Forming negative sentences in the past simple with didn't + base verb.

rules.t: Subject + didn't (did not) + base verb (not the past form!).
rules.td: Subjekt + didn't (did not) + Grundform (nicht die Vergangenheitsform!).

ex.en: I didn't go to school yesterday.
ex.de: Ich bin gestern nicht in die Schule gegangen.

errs.d: Using the past form after 'didn't' (double past marking — THE classic error)

structures.desc: Common irregular verbs that do not follow the -ed pattern in the past simple.

rules.t: Irregular verbs have special past forms that must be learnt by heart. They do NOT add -ed.
rules.td: unregelmäßige Verben haben besondere Vergangenheitsformen, die man auswendig lernen muss. Sie bekommen KEIN -ed.

negative: I didn't go to the park.
questions: Did you go to the party?

errs.w: I goed to the cinema yesterday.
errs.c: I went to the cinema yesterday.

errs.d: Regularising irregular verbs that change vowels

structures.desc: Talking about future plans and intentions with be + going to + base verb.

rules.t: Subject + am/is/are + going to + base verb.
rules.td: Subjekt + am/is/are + going to + Grundform.

ex.en: I'm going to visit my grandma.
She's going to travel to London.

rules.t: Negative: Subject + am not / isn't / aren't + going to + base verb.
rules.td: Verneinung: Subjekt + am not / isn't / aren't + going to + Grundform.

ex.en: I'm not going to watch TV.
They aren't going to play football.

rules.t: Questions: Am/Is/Are + subject + going to + base verb? Short answers: Yes, I am. / No, she isn't.
rules.td: Fragen: Am/Is/Are + Subjekt + going to + Grundform? Kurzantworten: Yes, I am. / No, she isn't.

ex.en: Are you going to come to the party? – Yes, I am.
ex.de: Wirst du zur Party kommen? – Ja.

ex.en: Is he going to study medicine? – No, he isn't.

affirmative: I'm going to buy a new phone.
She's going to read a book.
We're going to have a picnic.
negative: I'm not going to eat pizza.
questions: Are you going to help me?
Is she going to call us?

errs.w: I going to play football tomorrow.
errs.c: I'm going to play football tomorrow.

errs.d: Adding -ing to the main verb after 'going to'

items.p: I have got three ___ (cat).
items.h: Add -s to make most nouns plural.
items.hd: Hänge -s an, um die Mehrzahl zu bilden.
items.e: Most nouns form the plural by adding -s: cat → cats.
items.ed: Die meisten Nomen bilden die Mehrzahl mit -s: cat → cats.

items.p: There are five ___ (bus) at the bus stop.
items.h: Nouns ending in -s add -es for the plural.
items.hd: Nomen auf -s bekommen -es in der Mehrzahl.
items.e: Nouns ending in -s, -ss, -sh, -ch, -x add -es: bus → buses.
items.ed: Nomen auf -s, -ss, -sh, -ch, -x bekommen -es: bus → buses.

items.p: There are two ___ (baby) in the park.
items.h: When a noun ends in consonant + y, change y to i and add -es.
items.hd: Wenn ein Nomen auf Konsonant + y endet, wird y zu i und -es wird angehaengt.
items.e: Baby ends in consonant + y, so change y → i and add -es: babies.
items.ed: Baby endet auf Konsonant + y, daher wird y → i und -es angehaengt: babies.

items.p: I can see five ___ (child) in the playground.
items.h: Child has an irregular plural — learn it by heart!
items.hd: Child hat eine unregelmäßige Mehrzahl — lerne sie auswendig!
items.e: 'Child' is irregular: child → children. Do not add -s.
items.ed: 'Child' ist unregelmäßig: child → children. Kein -s anhängen.

items.p: We need two ___ (box) for the books.
items.h: Nouns ending in -x add -es.
items.hd: Nomen auf -x bekommen -es.
items.e: Nouns ending in -x add -es: box → boxes.
items.ed: Nomen auf -x bekommen -es: box → boxes.

items.p: The two ___ (woman) are my teachers.
items.h: Woman has an irregular plural — think of man → men.
items.hd: Woman hat eine unregelmäßige Mehrzahl — denke an man → men.
items.e: 'Woman' is irregular: woman → women (the vowel changes).
items.ed: 'Woman' ist unregelmäßig: woman → women (der Vokal ändert sich).

items.p: Choose the correct plural: Two ___
items.h: Mouse has an irregular plural.
items.hd: Mouse hat eine unregelmäßige Mehrzahl.
items.e: 'Mouse' is irregular: mouse → mice.
items.ed: 'Mouse' ist unregelmäßig: mouse → mice.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: There are many cities in Austria.
a: There are many cities in Austria.
ds: There are many citys in Austria.
There are many cityes in Austria.
There are many cityies in Austria.
items.h: City ends in consonant + y. What happens to the y?
items.hd: City endet auf Konsonant + y. Was passiert mit dem y?
items.e: Consonant + y → change y to i and add -es: city → cities.
items.ed: Konsonant + y → y wird zu i und -es wird angehaengt: city → cities.

items.p: Which plural is WRONG?
items.h: One of these is not a real English plural.
items.hd: Eine dieser Formen ist keine richtige englische Mehrzahl.
items.e: 'Tooth' → 'teeth' (not 'tooths'). It is an irregular plural.
items.ed: 'Tooth' → 'teeth' (nicht 'tooths'). Es ist eine unregelmäßige Mehrzahl.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: My foots are cold.
items.h: Is 'foots' the correct plural of 'foot'?
items.hd: Ist 'foots' die richtige Mehrzahl von 'foot'?
items.e: 'Foot' has an irregular plural: foot → feet (not 'foots').
items.ed: 'Foot' hat eine unregelmäßige Mehrzahl: foot → feet (nicht 'foots').

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: There are many babys in the hospital.
items.c: There are many babies in the hospital.
a: There are many babies in the hospital.
items.h: What happens when a noun ends in consonant + y?
items.hd: Was passiert, wenn ein Nomen auf Konsonant + y endet?
items.e: Baby ends in consonant + y → change y to i and add -es: babies.
items.ed: Baby endet auf Konsonant + y → y wird zu i und -es: babies.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I have two Dogs at home.
items.c: I have two dogs at home.
a: I have two dogs at home.
items.h: In English, are nouns capitalised like in German?
items.hd: Werden im Englischen Nomen grossgeschrieben wie im Deutschen?
items.e: In English, common nouns are NOT capitalised. Only proper nouns get capitals.
items.ed: Im Englischen werden gewöhnliche Nomen NICHT grossgeschrieben. Nur Eigennamen.

items.p: Your teacher asks you to say the plural. She says: 'one child, two...' Write the plural: one child → two ___
items.h: Child is an irregular noun.
items.hd: Child ist ein unregelmäßiges Nomen.
items.e: Child → children. This is an irregular plural — learn it by heart.
items.ed: Child → children. Das ist eine unregelmäßige Mehrzahl.

items.p: You are ordering food at the school canteen. How many do you want? Write the plural: one sandwich → four ___
items.h: Sandwich ends in -ch. What ending do you add?
items.hd: Sandwich endet auf -ch. Welche Endung kommt dazu?
items.e: Nouns ending in -ch add -es: sandwich → sandwiches.
items.ed: Nomen auf -ch bekommen -es: sandwich → sandwiches.

items.p: Translate into English: Ich habe drei Bücher.
items.h: How do you say 'Buecher' in English?
items.hd: Wie sagt man 'Bücher' auf Englisch?
items.e: 'Buch' = book, 'Buecher' = books (regular plural with -s).
items.ed: 'Buch' = book, 'Bücher' = books (regelmäßige Mehrzahl mit -s).

items.p: 🇩🇪 Es gibt viele Kinder im Park.
items.c: There are many children in the park.
a: There are many children in the park.
There are a lot of children in the park.
There are many kids in the park.
There are a lot of kids in the park.
There are lots of children in the park.
There are lots of kids in the park.
items.h: What is the plural of 'child'?
items.hd: Was ist die Mehrzahl von 'child'?
items.e: 'Kinder' = children (irregular). 'Es gibt' = 'there are' (plural).
items.ed: 'Kinder' = children (unregelmäßig). 'Es gibt' = 'there are' (Mehrzahl).

items.p: There are three ___ (man) in the garden.
items.h: Man has an irregular plural.
items.hd: Man hat eine unregelmäßige Mehrzahl.
items.e: 'Man' → 'men'. This is one of the most common irregular plurals.
items.ed: 'Man' → 'men'. Das ist eine der häufigsten unregelmäßigen Mehrzahlformen.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: are / six / there / in / fish / the / pond
items.c: There are six fish in the pond.
a: There are six fish in the pond.
items.h: Start with 'There are' for plural nouns.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'There are' bei Mehrzahl-Nomen.
items.e: 'Fish' is the same in singular and plural. 'There are' is used for plural.
items.ed: 'Fish' ist in Einzahl und Mehrzahl gleich. 'There are' wird bei der Mehrzahl verwendet.

items.p: Match the singular form to its correct plural: 1) foot 2) child 3) mouse 4) woman — a) women b) feet c) children d) mice
items.h: These are all irregular plurals — the words change completely.
items.hd: Das sind alles unregelmäßige Mehrzahlformen — die Wörter ändern sich komplett.
items.e: foot→feet, child→children, mouse→mice, woman→women. All irregular.
items.ed: foot→feet, child→children, mouse→mice, woman→women. Alle unregelmäßig.

items.p: ___ your books, please..
items.h: For commands, use the base form of the verb (no 'to').
items.hd: Für Befehle verwendest du die Grundform des Verbs (ohne 'to').
items.e: Imperatives use the base form: Open your books. No subject, no 'to'.
items.ed: Imperative verwenden die Grundform: Open your books. Kein Subjekt, kein 'to'.

items.p: ___ down, please..
items.h: Imperatives don't need 'to' or a subject.
items.hd: Imperative brauchen kein 'to' und kein Subjekt.
items.e: Just use the base form for commands: Sit down. No 'to', no 'you'.
items.ed: Verwende einfach die Grundform für Befehle: Sit down. Kein 'to', kein 'you'.

items.p: ___ run in the corridor! (negative command)
items.h: For negative commands, use 'Don't' + base verb.
items.hd: Für negative Befehle: 'Don't' + Grundform.
items.e: Negative imperative = Don't + base form: Don't run!
items.ed: Negativer Imperativ = Don't + Grundform: Don't run!

items.p: ___ to the teacher!.
items.h: Commands use the base form — no extras.
items.hd: Befehle verwenden die Grundform — nichts Zusätzliches.
items.e: Imperative = base form: Listen to the teacher!
items.ed: Imperativ = Grundform: Listen to the teacher!

items.p: ___ speak German in English class! (negative command)
items.h: What word do we use for negative commands?
items.hd: Welches Wort verwenden wir für negative Befehle?
items.e: Negative imperatives always use 'Don't' + base form.
items.ed: Negative Imperative verwenden immer 'Don't' + Grundform.

items.p: ___ the door, please. It's very hot. (open — positive command)
items.h: A polite command still uses the base form.
items.hd: Ein hoeflicher Befehl verwendet trotzdem die Grundform.
items.e: Open the door, please. Imperatives use the base verb only.
items.ed: Open the door, please. Imperative verwenden nur die Grundform.

items.p: The teacher says: '___ your books to page 10.'
items.h: The teacher is giving a command.
items.hd: Der Lehrer gibt einen Befehl.
items.e: Commands use the base form: Open your books.
items.ed: Befehle verwenden die Grundform: Open your books.

items.p: Which is a correct negative command?
items.h: Negative commands start with 'Don't'.
items.hd: Negative Befehle beginnen mit 'Don't'.
items.e: The correct pattern is: Don't + base form. Don't touch that!
items.ed: Das richtige Muster: Don't + Grundform. Don't touch that!

items.p: Which sentence is an imperative (command)?
items.h: An imperative has no subject and uses the base form.
items.hd: Ein Imperativ hat kein Subjekt und verwendet die Grundform.
items.e: 'Close the window.' is a command (imperative) — no subject, base form.
items.ed: 'Close the window.' ist ein Befehl (Imperativ) — kein Subjekt, Grundform.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Not speak in the library!
a: Do not speak in the library!
items.h: How do we make negative commands in English?
items.hd: Wie bildet man negative Befehle auf Englisch?
items.e: Negative commands use 'Don't' + base form, not just 'Not'.
items.ed: Negative Befehle verwenden 'Don't' + Grundform, nicht nur 'Not'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: To sit down, please.
items.h: Do imperatives use 'to' before the verb?
items.hd: Verwenden Imperative 'to' vor dem Verb?
items.e: Imperatives use the base form without 'to': Sit down, please.
items.ed: Imperative verwenden die Grundform ohne 'to': Sit down, please.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: You stand up now!
items.h: Do imperatives need a subject?
items.hd: Brauchen Imperative ein Subjekt?
items.e: Imperatives don't include a subject. Just say: Stand up!
items.ed: Imperative haben kein Subjekt. Sag einfach: Stand up!

items.p: Make this a negative command: Run in the corridor! → ___
a: Do not run in the corridor!
items.h: Add 'Don't' before the base verb.
items.hd: Setze 'Don't' vor die Grundform.
items.e: To make a negative command, add 'Don't' before the verb: Don't run!
items.ed: Für einen negativen Befehl setze 'Don't' vor das Verb: Don't run!

items.p: Make this a positive command: You must not eat in class. → ___
items.h: Turn 'You must not...' into a negative command.
items.hd: Verwandle 'You must not...' in einen negativen Befehl.
items.e: 'You must not eat' becomes the imperative 'Don't eat in class!'
items.ed: 'You must not eat' wird zum Imperativ: 'Don't eat in class!'

items.p: 🇩🇪 Öffne das Fenster!
items.h: Use the base form for commands in English.
items.hd: Verwende die Grundform für Befehle auf Englisch.
items.e: 'Oeffne' = command → base form 'Open'. Open the window!
items.ed: 'Öffne' = Befehl → Grundform 'Open'. Open the window!

items.p: 🇩🇪 Lauf nicht!
items.h: How do you say 'nicht' in a command?
items.hd: Wie sagt man 'nicht' in einem Befehl?
items.e: German 'nicht' in commands = 'Don't' in English: Don't run!
items.ed: Deutsches 'nicht' in Befehlen = 'Don't' auf Englisch: Don't run!

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: the / don't / window / open
items.h: Negative commands start with 'Don't'.
items.hd: Negative Befehle beginnen mit 'Don't'.
items.e: Don't + base verb + object: Don't open the window.
items.ed: Don't + Grundform + Objekt: Don't open the window.

items.p: Match the command to the correct situation: 1) Open the window. 2) Don't run! 3) Sit down. 4) Don't eat in class. — a) in the corridor b) it's hot in the room c) at the start of a lesson d) school canteen rule
items.h: Think about when and where each command is used.
items.hd: Überleg, wann und wo jeder Befehl verwendet wird.
items.e: Open the window (hot room), Don't run (corridor), Sit down (lesson start), Don't eat (class rule).
items.ed: Open the window (heisser Raum), Don't run (Gang), Sit down (Stundenbeginn), Don't eat (Klassenregel).

items.p: ___ from Vienna. (I am — use the short form)
items.h: I am = I'm. Don't forget the apostrophe!
items.hd: I am = I'm. Vergiss den Apostroph nicht!
items.e: I am becomes I'm. The apostrophe replaces the missing letter 'a'.
items.ed: I am wird zu I'm. Der Apostroph ersetzt den fehlenden Buchstaben 'a'.

items.p: ___ your name? (What is — use the short form)
items.h: What is = What's
items.hd: What is = What's
items.e: What is becomes What's. The apostrophe replaces 'i'.
items.ed: What is wird zu What's. Der Apostroph ersetzt das 'i'.

items.p: ___ a sunny day today. (It is — use the short form)
items.h: It is = It's. Don't confuse with 'its' (possession).
items.hd: It is = It's. Nicht verwechseln mit 'its' (Besitz).
items.e: 'It's' = it is. 'Its' (no apostrophe) = possessive.
items.ed: 'It's' = it is. 'Its' (ohne Apostroph) = Besitz.

items.p: That ___ right. (is not — use the short form)
items.h: is not = isn't. Where does the apostrophe go?
items.hd: is not = isn't. Wo kommt der Apostroph hin?
items.e: is not = isn't. The apostrophe replaces the 'o' in 'not'.
items.ed: is not = isn't. Der Apostroph ersetzt das 'o' in 'not'.

items.p: ___ go to the park! (Let us — use the short form)
items.h: Let us = Let's
items.hd: Let us = Let's
items.e: Let us = Let's. Used for suggestions: Let's go!
items.ed: Let us = Let's. Wird für Vorschlaege verwendet: Let's go!

items.p: The cat is eating ___ food. (belonging to it — NO apostrophe!)
items.h: 'Its' (no apostrophe) means 'belonging to it'.
items.hd: 'Its' (ohne Apostroph) bedeutet 'gehört ihm/ihr (Sache/Tier)'.
items.e: Possessive 'its' has NO apostrophe. 'It's' = 'it is'.
items.ed: Das besitzanzeigende 'its' hat KEINEN Apostroph. 'It's' = 'it is'.

items.p: Choose the correct contraction for 'I am':
items.h: The apostrophe shows where a letter is missing.
items.hd: Der Apostroph zeigt, wo ein Buchstabe fehlt.
items.e: I am → I'm. The apostrophe replaces the 'a'.
items.ed: I am → I'm. Der Apostroph ersetzt das 'a'.

items.p: Which sentence uses 'it's' correctly?
items.h: It's = it is. Its = belonging to it.
items.hd: It's = it is. Its = gehört ihm/ihr.
items.e: 'It's a beautiful day' = 'It is a beautiful day.' The others need 'its' (possessive).
items.ed: 'It's a beautiful day' = 'It is a beautiful day.' Die anderen brauchen 'its' (Besitz).

items.p: Which is the correct contraction of 'is not'?
items.h: The apostrophe replaces the letter 'o' in 'not'.
items.hd: Der Apostroph ersetzt den Buchstaben 'o' in 'not'.
items.e: is + not = isn't. The 'o' is removed and replaced by an apostrophe.
items.ed: is + not = isn't. Das 'o' wird entfernt und durch einen Apostroph ersetzt.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Im from Austria.
items.h: Is there a missing punctuation mark?
items.hd: Fehlt ein Satzzeichen?
items.e: 'Im' is wrong — it must be 'I'm' (with apostrophe) = I am.
items.ed: 'Im' ist falsch — es muss 'I'm' (mit Apostroph) heissen = I am.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The cat is eating it's food.
items.c: The cat is eating its food.
a: The cat is eating its food.
items.h: Does 'it's' mean 'it is' or 'belonging to it' here?
items.hd: Bedeutet 'it's' hier 'it is' oder 'gehört ihm/ihr'?
items.e: Here we mean 'belonging to it' → 'its' (no apostrophe). 'It's' = it is.
items.ed: Hier meinen wir 'gehört ihm/ihr' → 'its' (ohne Apostroph). 'It's' = it is.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Whats your favourite colour?
items.h: Is there a missing apostrophe?
items.hd: Fehlt ein Apostroph?
items.e: 'Whats' needs an apostrophe: What's = What is.
items.ed: 'Whats' braucht einen Apostroph: What's = What is.

items.p: You are texting your friend and want to be quick. Write the short form: I am happy. → ___
items.h: I am = I'm
items.hd: I am = I'm
items.e: I am → I'm. The apostrophe replaces the 'a'.
items.ed: I am → I'm. Der Apostroph ersetzt das 'a'.

items.p: Your teacher wants the full sentence on the test. Write the long form: It isn't cold. → ___
items.h: isn't = is not
items.hd: isn't = is not
items.e: isn't = is not. It isn't cold = It is not cold.
items.ed: isn't = is not. It isn't cold = It is not cold.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich bin aus Graz. (use the short form)
items.h: I am = I'm. 'aus' = 'from'.
items.hd: I am = I'm. 'aus' = 'from'.
items.e: 'Ich bin' = 'I am' = 'I'm'. 'aus Graz' = 'from Graz'.
items.ed: 'Ich bin' = 'I am' = 'I'm'. 'aus Graz' = 'from Graz'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Es ist nicht kalt heute. (use contractions)
items.h: 'Es ist nicht' = 'It isn't' or 'It's not'.
items.hd: 'Es ist nicht' = 'It isn't' oder 'It's not'.
items.e: 'Es ist nicht kalt' = 'It isn't cold' or 'It's not cold'.
items.ed: 'Es ist nicht kalt' = 'It isn't cold' oder 'It's not cold'.

items.p: The dog is wagging ___ tail happily.
items.h: 'Its' (no apostrophe) = belonging to it. 'It's' = it is.
items.hd: 'Its' (ohne Apostroph) = gehört ihm/ihr. 'It's' = it is.
items.e: 'Its' is the possessive form. No apostrophe! 'It's' = it is.
items.ed: 'Its' ist die besitzanzeigende Form. Kein Apostroph! 'It's' = it is.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: name / What's / your
items.h: Start with the question word.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Fragewort.
items.e: What's your name? = What is your name?
items.ed: What's your name? = What is your name?

items.p: Match the long form to the short form: 1) I am 2) it is 3) is not 4) Let us — a) isn't b) Let's c) I'm d) it's
items.h: The apostrophe shows where letters are missing.
items.hd: Der Apostroph zeigt, wo Buchstaben fehlen.
items.e: I am→I'm, it is→it's, is not→isn't, Let us→Let's.
items.ed: I am→I'm, it is→it's, is not→isn't, Let us→Let's.

items.p: I ___ twelve years old. (be)
items.h: I + ___ ? Which form of 'be' goes with 'I'?
items.hd: I + ___ ? Welche Form von 'be' passt zu 'I'?
items.e: 'I' always takes 'am': I am twelve years old.
items.ed: 'I' nimmt immer 'am': I am twelve years old.

items.p: She ___ from Vienna. (be)
items.h: 'She' is 3rd person singular — he/she/it + ___?
items.hd: 'She' ist 3. Person Einzahl — he/she/it + ___?
items.e: 'She' takes 'is': She is from Vienna.
items.ed: 'She' nimmt 'is': She is from Vienna.

items.p: Tom and Lisa ___ in class 1A. (be)
items.h: Tom and Lisa = they. They + ___?
items.hd: Tom and Lisa = they. They + ___?
items.e: Two people = plural = 'are': Tom and Lisa are in class 1A.
items.ed: Zwei Personen = Mehrzahl = 'are': Tom and Lisa are in class 1A.

items.p: He ___ at home today. He is sick. (not / be)
items.h: He = 3rd person singular. How do you make 'is' negative?
items.hd: He = 3. Person Einzahl. Wie verneint man 'is'?
items.e: 'He' + negative 'be' = isn't: He isn't at home.
items.ed: 'He' + verneinetes 'be' = isn't: He isn't at home.

items.p: ___ you happy? — Yes, I ___. (be questions & short answers)
items.h: To make questions with 'be', swap subject and verb. Short answer: Yes, I am.
items.hd: Für Fragen mit 'be' tausche Subjekt und Verb. Kurzantwort: Yes, I am.
items.e: Are you happy? — Yes, I am. (No contraction in affirmative short answers.)
items.ed: Are you happy? — Yes, I am. (Keine Kurzform in positiven Kurzantworten.)

items.p: They ___ my friends.
items.h: They = plural. Which form of 'be'?
items.hd: They = Mehrzahl. Welche Form von 'be'?
items.e: 'They' takes 'are': They are my friends.
items.ed: 'They' nimmt 'are': They are my friends.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.h: 'We' is plural — like 'they'.
items.hd: 'We' ist Mehrzahl — wie 'they'.
items.e: 'We' takes 'are': We are from Graz.
items.ed: 'We' nimmt 'are': We are from Graz.

items.p: Which short answer is correct? — 'Is she your sister?' — '___'
items.h: Short answers repeat the auxiliary verb (am/is/are). Can you use a contraction in a 'yes' answer?
items.hd: Kurze Antworten wiederholen das Hilfsverb. Kann man in einer 'yes'-Antwort eine Kurzform verwenden?
items.e: Short answers with 'be' use 'be': No, she isn't. Never use 'do' with 'be'.
items.ed: Kurzantworten mit 'be' verwenden 'be': No, she isn't. Nie 'do' mit 'be'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He are a teacher.
items.h: 'He' = 3rd person singular. Which form of 'be'?
items.hd: 'He' = 3. Person Einzahl. Welche Form von 'be'?
items.e: 'He' takes 'is', not 'are': He is a teacher.
items.ed: 'He' nimmt 'is', nicht 'are': He is a teacher.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Do you are happy?
items.h: Questions with 'be' don't use 'do'.
items.hd: Fragen mit 'be' verwenden kein 'do'.
items.e: With 'be', just swap subject and verb: Are you happy? (not 'Do you are')
items.ed: Bei 'be' tausche einfach Subjekt und Verb: Are you happy? (nicht 'Do you are')

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Are you from Salzburg? — Yes, I'm.
items.c: Are you from Salzburg? — Yes, I am.
a: Are you from Salzburg? — Yes, I am.
Are you from Salzburg? — Yes, I am
items.h: Can you use a contraction in a 'yes' short answer?
items.hd: Kann man in einer 'yes'-Kurzantwort eine Kurzform verwenden?
items.e: You cannot contract 'yes' short answers: 'Yes, I am.' (not 'Yes, I'm.')
items.ed: Positive Kurzantworten darf man nicht verkürzen: 'Yes, I am.' (nicht 'Yes, I'm.')

items.p: Your friend says 'I am tired' but it's not true for you. Make this negative: I am tired. → I ___ tired.
items.h: 'I am' → negative = 'I am not' or 'I'm not'.
items.hd: 'I am' → Verneinung = 'I am not' oder 'I'm not'.
items.e: I am → I am not / I'm not. (There is no 'amn't'.)
items.ed: I am → I am not / I'm not. (Es gibt kein 'amn't'.)

items.p: You want to check if your new classmate is from London. Make this a question: She is from London. → ___ she from London?
items.h: Swap subject and 'be' to form a question.
items.hd: Tausche Subjekt und 'be' für eine Frage.
items.e: She is → Is she? Just swap the subject and 'be'.
items.ed: She is → Is she? Tausche einfach Subjekt und 'be'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wir sind in der 1A.
items.h: 'Wir sind' = 'We are'.
items.hd: 'Wir sind' = 'We are'.
items.e: 'Wir' = 'We', 'sind' = 'are'. We are in class 1A.
items.ed: 'Wir' = 'We', 'sind' = 'are'. We are in class 1A.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Bist du muede? — Nein, bin ich nicht.
items.c: Are you tired? — No, I'm not.
a: Are you tired? — No, I'm not.
Are you tired? — No, I am not.
Are you tired? — No, I'm not
items.h: 'Bist du' = 'Are you'. Negative short answer: 'No, I'm not.'
items.hd: 'Bist du' = 'Are you'. Negative Kurzantwort: 'No, I'm not.'
items.e: 'Bist du muede?' = 'Are you tired?' Short answer: No, I'm not.
items.ed: 'Bist du muede?' = 'Are you tired?' Kurzantwort: No, I'm not.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: from / are / they / Salzburg
items.h: Start with the subject, then 'be', then the rest.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt, dann 'be', dann der Rest.
items.e: Subject + be + rest: They are from Salzburg.
items.ed: Subjekt + be + Rest: They are from Salzburg.

items.p: Match the subject to the correct form of 'be': 1) I 2) she 3) we 4) they — a) are b) am c) is d) are
items.h: I=am, he/she/it=is, you/we/they=are.
items.hd: I=am, he/she/it=is, you/we/they=are.
items.e: I am, she is, we are, they are.
items.ed: I am, she is, we are, they are.

items.p: Your friend asks about your sister. Which sentence uses 'to be' correctly?
items.h: She/he/it uses 'is'. I uses 'am'. You/we/they use 'are'.
items.hd: She/he/it braucht 'is'. I braucht 'am'. You/we/they brauchen 'are'.
items.e: 'She is' — use 'is' for he, she, it in present simple of 'be'.
items.ed: 'She is' — verwende 'is' für he, she, it im Present Simple von 'be'.

items.p: The book is ___ the table. (on a surface)
items.h: The book is touching the surface of the table.
items.hd: Das Buch beruehrt die Oberflaeche des Tisches.
items.e: 'On' means on a surface: The book is on the table.
items.ed: 'On' bedeutet auf einer Oberflaeche: The book is on the table.

items.p: The cat is ___ the box. (inside)
items.h: The cat is inside the box.
items.hd: Die Katze ist innerhalb der Schachtel.
items.e: 'In' means inside: The cat is in the box.
items.ed: 'In' bedeutet drinnen: The cat is in the box.

items.p: The dog is ___ the bed. (below it)
items.h: The dog is below the bed.
items.hd: Der Hund ist unterhalb des Bettes.
items.e: 'Under' means below or beneath: The dog is under the bed.
items.ed: 'Under' bedeutet unter/darunter: The dog is under the bed.

items.p: The tree is ___ ___ ___ the house. (facing the house)
items.h: 'Vor dem Haus' = 'in front of the house' — three words!
items.hd: 'Vor dem Haus' = 'in front of the house' — drei Wörter!
items.e: 'In front of' is a three-word preposition. Don't forget 'of'!
items.ed: 'In front of' ist eine dreiteilige Präposition. Vergiss 'of' nicht!

items.p: The supermarket is ___ ___ the school. (beside it)
items.h: 'Neben' = 'next to' — two words.
items.hd: 'Neben' = 'next to' — zwei Wörter.
items.e: 'Next to' means beside: The supermarket is next to the school.
items.ed: 'Next to' bedeutet neben: The supermarket is next to the school.

items.p: The children are ___ the tree. (at the back of the tree)
items.h: 'Behind' = 'dahinter'. It does NOT need 'of'.
items.hd: 'Behind' = 'dahinter'. Es braucht KEIN 'of'.
items.e: 'Behind' means at the back of. No 'of' needed (unlike 'in front of').
items.ed: 'Behind' bedeutet dahinter. Kein 'of' nötig (anders als 'in front of').

items.p: The pen is ___ my bag. (inside my bag)
items.h: Think about whether the pen is on the surface or inside the bag.
items.hd: Ist der Stift auf der Oberfläche oder im Inneren der Tasche?
items.e: 'In' = inside: The pen is in my bag.
items.ed: 'In' = drinnen: The pen is in my bag.

items.p: Which sentence is correct? The park is ___ the school.
items.h: The German word 'neben' translates to a two-word preposition in English.
items.hd: Das Wort 'neben' wird im Englischen mit zwei Wörtern übersetzt.
items.e: 'Next to' is always two words: The park is next to the school.
items.ed: 'Next to' besteht immer aus zwei Wörtern.

items.p: Which is correct? The bus stop is ___.
items.h: 'In front of' has three parts — don't forget any!
items.hd: 'In front of' hat drei Teile — vergiss keinen!
items.e: 'In front of' = vor. All three words are needed: in front of the school.
items.ed: 'In front of' = vor. Alle drei Wörter sind nötig.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The tree is in front the house.
items.c: The tree is in front of the house.
a: The tree is in front of the house.
items.h: Something is missing in 'in front ___ the house'.
items.hd: Es fehlt etwas in 'in front ___ the house'.
items.e: 'In front of' needs 'of': The tree is in front of the house.
items.ed: 'In front of' braucht 'of': The tree is in front of the house.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The shop is next the cinema.
items.c: The shop is next to the cinema.
a: The shop is next to the cinema.
items.h: 'Next' needs another word after it.
items.hd: 'Next' braucht noch ein Wort danach.
items.e: 'Next to' always needs 'to': The shop is next to the cinema.
items.ed: 'Next to' braucht immer 'to': The shop is next to the cinema.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I am at school at Monday.
items.c: I am at school on Monday.
a: I am at school on Monday.
items.h: Which preposition do we use with days of the week?
items.hd: Welche Präposition verwenden wir bei Wochentagen?
items.e: 'At school' (not 'in school' for being there) and 'on Monday' (not 'at Monday').
items.ed: 'At school' (nicht 'in school') und 'on Monday' (nicht 'at Monday').

items.p: Change the preposition to the opposite: The cat is under the table. → The cat is ___ the table.
a: The cat is on the table.
The cat is on the table
items.h: What is the opposite of 'under'?
items.hd: Was ist das Gegenteil von 'under'?
items.e: The opposite of 'under' (below) is 'on' (on the surface).
items.ed: Das Gegenteil von 'under' (darunter) ist 'on' (auf der Oberflaeche).

items.p: Change the preposition to the opposite: The car is behind the house. → The car is ___ ___ ___ the house.
a: The car is in front of in front of in front of the house.
The car is in front of in front of in front of the house
items.h: What is the opposite of 'behind'?
items.hd: Was ist das Gegenteil von 'behind'?
items.e: The opposite of 'behind' is 'in front of'.
items.ed: Das Gegenteil von 'behind' ist 'in front of'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Die Katze ist unter dem Bett.
items.c: The cat is under the bed.
a: The cat is under the bed.
items.h: 'unter' = 'under'.
items.hd: 'unter' = 'under'.
items.e: 'unter dem Bett' = 'under the bed'.
items.ed: 'unter dem Bett' = 'under the bed'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Das Auto ist vor dem Haus.
items.c: The car is in front of the house.
a: The car is in front of the house.
items.h: 'vor' = 'in front of' (three words!).
items.hd: 'vor' = 'in front of' (drei Wörter!).
items.e: 'vor dem Haus' = 'in front of the house'. Remember: three words!
items.ed: 'vor dem Haus' = 'in front of the house'. Denke daran: drei Wörter!

items.p: The cat is hiding ___ the sofa.
items.h: Use: in, on, under, behind, or next to.
items.hd: Verwende: in, on, under, behind oder next to.
items.e: Use a preposition to describe where things are: on, in, under, next to, behind.
items.ed: Verwende eine Präposition um zu beschreiben wo Dinge sind: on, in, under, next to, behind.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: the / is / behind / cat / door / the
items.c: The cat is behind the door.
a: The cat is behind the door.
items.h: Subject + is + preposition + the + noun.
items.hd: Subjekt + is + Präposition + the + Nomen.
items.e: The cat is behind the door. Subject + be + preposition + place.
items.ed: The cat is behind the door. Subjekt + be + Präposition + Ort.

items.p: Match the English preposition to the German word: 1) on 2) under 3) behind 4) next to — a) neben b) hinter c) auf d) unter
items.h: Think of the German translation for each preposition.
items.hd: Denke an die deutsche Übersetzung jeder Präposition.
items.e: on=auf, under=unter, behind=hinter, next to=neben.
items.ed: on=auf, under=unter, behind=hinter, next to=neben.

items.p: ___ a cat on the sofa. (there is / there are)
items.h: One cat = singular. Use 'there is' or 'there are'?
items.hd: Eine Katze = Einzahl. 'There is' oder 'there are'?
items.e: 'A cat' is singular → 'There is a cat on the sofa.'
items.ed: 'A cat' ist Einzahl → 'There is a cat on the sofa.'

items.p: ___ twenty students in our class. (there is / there are)
items.h: Twenty students = plural. Which do you use?
items.hd: Zwanzig Schueler = Mehrzahl. Was verwendest du?
items.e: 'Twenty students' is plural → 'There are twenty students.'
items.ed: 'Twenty students' ist Mehrzahl → 'There are twenty students.'

items.p: ___ three monkeys in the zoo. (there is / there are)
items.h: Three monkeys = plural!
items.hd: Drei Affen = Mehrzahl!
items.e: Plural nouns need 'there are': There are three monkeys.
items.ed: Mehrzahl-Nomen brauchen 'there are': There are three monkeys.

items.p: ___ a swimming pool in our town? (question form)
items.h: To make a question, swap 'there' and 'is/are'.
items.hd: Für eine Frage tausche 'there' und 'is/are'.
items.e: Question: Is there a swimming pool? (Swap 'there' and 'is'.)
items.ed: Frage: Is there a swimming pool? (Tausche 'there' und 'is'.)

items.p: ___ any parks near your house? (question, plural)
items.h: Parks = plural. Question form = swap 'there' and 'are'.
items.hd: Parks = Mehrzahl. Frageform = tausche 'there' und 'are'.
items.e: Plural question: Are there any parks near your house?
items.ed: Mehrzahl-Frage: Are there any parks near your house?

items.p: There ___ a big garden but there ___ any trees. (is/are, negative)
items.h: One garden = singular 'is'. Trees = plural 'aren't'.
items.hd: Ein Garten = Einzahl 'is'. Baeume = Mehrzahl 'aren't'.
items.e: There is a garden (singular) but there aren't any trees (plural).
items.ed: There is a garden (Einzahl) but there aren't any trees (Mehrzahl).

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: There are five dogs in the park.
a: There are five dogs in the park.
ds: There is five dogs in the park.
It has five dogs in the park.
It gives five dogs in the park.
items.h: Check: is the noun after 'there' singular or plural?
items.hd: Überprüfe: Ist das Nomen nach 'there' Einzahl oder Mehrzahl?
items.e: Five dogs = plural → 'There are'. Not 'it has' or 'it gives'!
items.ed: Fünf Hunde = Mehrzahl → 'There are'. Nicht 'it has' oder 'it gives'!

items.p: ___ a library in your school?
items.h: 'A library' = singular. This is a question.
items.hd: 'A library' = Einzahl. Das ist eine Frage.
items.e: Singular question: Is there a library? (Swap 'there' and 'is'.)
items.ed: Einzahl-Frage: Is there a library? (Tausche 'there' und 'is'.)

items.p: Which sentence is WRONG?
items.c: It has a park in our town.
a: It has a park in our town.
ds: There is a park in our town.
There's a park in our town.
There are parks in our town.
items.h: One of these is a direct translation from German 'es gibt'.
items.hd: Einer davon ist eine direkte Übersetzung von 'es gibt'.
items.e: 'It has a park' is wrong. German 'es gibt' = 'there is/are' in English, NOT 'it has'.
items.ed: 'It has a park' ist falsch. 'Es gibt' = 'there is/are' auf Englisch, NICHT 'it has'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: There is many children in the zoo.
items.c: There are many children in the zoo.
a: There are many children in the zoo.
items.h: 'Many children' is plural. Which form do you need?
items.hd: 'Many children' ist Mehrzahl. Welche Form brauchst du?
items.e: 'Many children' is plural → 'There are many children.'
items.ed: 'Many children' ist Mehrzahl → 'There are many children.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: It has a big park in our town.
items.c: There is a big park in our town.
a: There is a big park in our town.
There's a big park in our town.
items.h: 'Es gibt' = 'there is/are', NOT 'it has'.
items.hd: 'Es gibt' = 'there is/are', NICHT 'it has'.
items.e: German 'es gibt' = 'there is'. 'It has a park' is a direct L1 translation error.
items.ed: Deutsches 'es gibt' = 'there is'. 'It has a park' ist ein L1-Übersetzungsfehler.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: There is two bedrooms and a big kitchen.
items.c: There are two bedrooms and a big kitchen.
a: There are two bedrooms and a big kitchen.
items.h: The first noun after 'there is/are' decides: 'two bedrooms' = ?
items.hd: Das erste Nomen nach 'there is/are' entscheidet: 'two bedrooms' = ?
items.e: The verb agrees with the first noun: 'two bedrooms' = plural → 'there are'.
items.ed: Das Verb richtet sich nach dem ersten Nomen: 'two bedrooms' = Mehrzahl → 'there are'.

items.p: A friend says there's a cinema in your town, but there isn't one! Make this negative: There is a cinema in our town. → There ___ a cinema in our town.
a: There isn't a cinema in our town.
There isn't a cinema in our town
There is not a cinema in our town.
items.h: 'There is' → negative = 'there isn't'.
items.hd: 'There is' → Verneinung = 'there isn't'.
items.e: There is → There isn't: There isn't a cinema in our town.
items.ed: There is → There isn't: There isn't a cinema in our town.

items.p: Make this a question: There are shops near the school. → ___ there shops near the school?
a: Are there shops near the school?
Are there shops near the school
items.h: Swap 'there' and 'are' to make a question.
items.hd: Tausche 'there' und 'are' für eine Frage.
items.e: There are → Are there? Swap to form a question.
items.ed: There are → Are there? Tausche für eine Frage.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Es gibt einen Park in unserer Stadt.
items.c: There is a park in our town.
a: There is a park in our town.
There's a park in our town.
items.h: 'Es gibt' = 'there is/are'. A park = singular.
items.hd: 'Es gibt' = 'there is/are'. Ein Park = Einzahl.
items.e: 'Es gibt' = 'there is' (singular). There is a park in our town.
items.ed: 'Es gibt' = 'there is' (Einzahl). There is a park in our town.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Gibt es ein Schwimmbad in deiner Stadt?
items.c: Is there a swimming pool in your town?
a: Is there a swimming pool in your town?
Is there a pool in your town?
items.h: 'Gibt es' = question form of 'there is'. Swap 'is' and 'there'.
items.hd: 'Gibt es' = Frageform von 'there is'. Tausche 'is' und 'there'.
items.e: 'Gibt es' = 'Is there' (question). Is there a swimming pool?
items.ed: 'Gibt es' = 'Is there' (Frage). Is there a swimming pool?

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: are / in / students / there / the / twenty / class
items.c: There are twenty students in the class.
a: There are twenty students in the class.
items.h: Start with 'There are' because 'twenty students' is plural.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'There are', weil 'twenty students' Mehrzahl ist.
items.e: There are + number + plural noun + place.
items.ed: There are + Zahl + Mehrzahl-Nomen + Ort.

items.p: Match the beginning to the correct ending: 1) There is 2) There are 3) Is there 4) Are there — a) any shops near here? b) a cat in the garden. c) three books on the table. d) a cinema in town?
items.h: 'Is' goes with singular, 'are' goes with plural.
items.hd: 'Is' passt zu Einzahl, 'are' passt zu Mehrzahl.
items.e: There is + singular, There are + plural. Questions: Is there...? Are there...?
items.ed: There is + Einzahl, There are + Mehrzahl. Fragen: Is there...? Are there...?

items.p: Maria is my friend. ___ is very nice. (he / she / it)
items.h: Maria is a girl. Which pronoun for a girl?
items.hd: Maria ist ein Mädchen. Welches Pronomen für ein Mädchen?
items.e: Maria is female → 'she'. Never use 'it' for people.
items.ed: Maria ist weiblich → 'she'. Verwende nie 'it' für Menschen.

items.p: Tom and Anna are friends. ___ are in Year 7. (he / she / they)
items.h: Tom and Anna = two people. Which pronoun for more than one person?
items.hd: Tom und Anna = zwei Personen. Welches Pronomen für mehrere Personen?
items.e: Tom and Anna = they (3rd person plural).
items.ed: Tom und Anna = they (3. Person Mehrzahl).

items.p: The book is new. ___ is very interesting. (he / she / it)
items.h: In English, objects and things are always 'it'.
items.hd: Im Englischen sind Gegenstaende und Dinge immer 'it'.
items.e: 'Book' is a thing → 'it'. English has no grammatical gender for objects.
items.ed: 'Book' ist eine Sache → 'it'. Englisch hat kein grammatisches Geschlecht für Objekte.

items.p: The table is big. ___ is brown. (he / she / it)
items.h: German: 'der Tisch' (masculine). But in English, all things = 'it'.
items.hd: Deutsch: 'der Tisch' (maennlich). Aber auf Englisch sind alle Dinge = 'it'.
items.e: In English, things are always 'it', even if they are masculine in German.
items.ed: Auf Englisch sind Dinge immer 'it', auch wenn sie im Deutschen maennlich sind.

items.p: My sister and I love sports. ___ play tennis every week. (we / they / you)
items.h: 'My sister and I' = we (I am included!).
items.hd: 'My sister and I' = we (ich bin dabei!).
items.e: When 'I' is part of the group, use 'we': My sister and I → We.
items.ed: Wenn 'I' Teil der Gruppe ist, verwende 'we': My sister and I → We.

items.p: Where is the dog? ___ is in the garden. (he / she / it)
items.h: Animals are usually 'it' unless you know the gender.
items.hd: Tiere sind normalerweise 'it', es sei denn, man kennt das Geschlecht.
items.e: For animals (when gender is unknown), use 'it'. If it's your pet, he/she is OK too.
items.ed: Für Tiere (wenn Geschlecht unbekannt) verwende 'it'. Beim eigenen Haustier geht he/she auch.

items.p: Max is in class 1B. ___ is from Linz.
items.h: Max is a boy.
items.hd: Max ist ein Bub.
items.e: Max is male → 'He'. Never use 'it' for people.
items.ed: Max ist maennlich → 'He'. Verwende nie 'it' für Menschen.

items.p: The door is old. ___ is brown.
items.h: In English, objects don't have gender.
items.hd: Im Englischen haben Gegenstaende kein Geschlecht.
items.e: 'Door' is a thing → always 'it' in English.
items.ed: 'Door' ist eine Sache → immer 'it' auf Englisch.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: This is my teacher. She is very nice.
a: This is my teacher. She is very nice.
ds: This is my teacher. It is very nice.
This is my teacher. He is very nice she.
This is my teacher. Her is very nice.
items.h: People are always 'he' or 'she', never 'it'.
items.hd: Menschen sind immer 'he' oder 'she', nie 'it'.
items.e: For people, use 'he' or 'she'. 'She' = subject, 'her' = object.
items.ed: Für Menschen verwende 'he' oder 'she'. 'She' = Subjekt, 'her' = Objekt.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The table is big. He is brown.
items.c: The table is big. It is brown.
a: The table is big. It is brown.
The table is big. It's brown.
The table is big. It is brown
items.h: Is 'the table' a person or a thing?
items.hd: Ist 'the table' eine Person oder eine Sache?
items.e: 'Table' is a thing → 'it', not 'he'. German 'der Tisch' is masculine, but English doesn't use gender for objects.
items.ed: 'Table' ist eine Sache → 'it', nicht 'he'. Deutsch 'der Tisch' ist maennlich, aber Englisch hat kein Geschlecht für Dinge.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: This is my brother. It is eleven.
items.c: This is my brother. He is eleven.
a: This is my brother. He is eleven.
This is my brother. He's eleven.
This is my brother. He is eleven
items.h: Is 'my brother' a person or a thing?
items.hd: Ist 'my brother' eine Person oder eine Sache?
items.e: 'My brother' is a male person → 'he'. Never use 'it' for people.
items.ed: 'My brother' ist eine maennliche Person → 'he'. Verwende nie 'it' für Menschen.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The bag is new. She is very nice.
items.c: The bag is new. It is very nice.
a: The bag is new. It is very nice.
The bag is new. It's very nice.
The bag is new. It is very nice
items.h: 'Die Tasche' is feminine in German. But in English?
items.hd: 'Die Tasche' ist im Deutschen weiblich. Aber auf Englisch?
items.e: German 'die Tasche' is feminine, but in English things are always 'it'.
items.ed: Deutsch 'die Tasche' ist weiblich, aber auf Englisch sind Dinge immer 'it'.

items.p: Replace the noun with a pronoun: Lisa is happy. → ___ is happy.
items.h: Lisa is female.
items.hd: Lisa ist weiblich.
items.e: Lisa → She. Female names use the pronoun 'she'.
items.ed: Lisa → She. Weibliche Namen verwenden das Pronomen 'she'.

items.p: Replace the nouns with pronouns: The children are tired. → ___ are tired.
items.h: 'The children' = more than one person.
items.hd: 'The children' = mehr als eine Person.
items.e: 'The children' = plural people → 'they'.
items.ed: 'The children' = Mehrzahl Personen → 'they'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er ist mein Freund. (male friend)
items.h: 'Er' = 'He'.
items.hd: 'Er' = 'He'.
items.e: 'Er' = 'He'. He is my friend.
items.ed: 'Er' = 'He'. He is my friend.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Der Stuhl ist alt. Er ist braun. (Chair is not a person!)
items.c: The chair is old. It is brown.
a: The chair is old. It is brown.
The chair is old. It's brown.
The chair is old. It is brown
items.h: German 'der Stuhl' is masculine, but in English, things are always 'it'.
items.hd: Deutsch 'der Stuhl' ist maennlich, aber Englisch: Dinge = 'it'.
items.e: Even though German uses 'er' (he) for 'der Stuhl', English uses 'it' for all things.
items.ed: Obwohl Deutsch 'er' für 'der Stuhl' verwendet, benutzt Englisch 'it' für alle Dinge.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: is / she / my / sister
items.h: Subject pronoun first, then verb, then the rest.
items.hd: Subjektpronomen zuerst, dann Verb, dann der Rest.
items.e: She is my sister. Subject + be + complement.
items.ed: She is my sister. Subjekt + be + Ergaenzung.

items.p: Match the noun to the correct pronoun: 1) Tom 2) Lisa 3) Tom and Lisa 4) the book — a) they b) it c) he d) she
items.h: Male=he, female=she, more than one=they, thing=it.
items.hd: Maennlich=he, weiblich=she, mehrere=they, Sache=it.
items.e: Tom=he, Lisa=she, Tom and Lisa=they, the book=it.
items.ed: Tom=he, Lisa=she, Tom und Lisa=they, the book=it.

items.p: I ___ got a new bike. (have / has)
items.h: I/you/we/they + have got. He/she/it + has got.
items.hd: I/you/we/they + have got. He/she/it + has got.
items.e: 'I' takes 'have': I have got a new bike.
items.ed: 'I' nimmt 'have': I have got a new bike.

items.p: She ___ got blue eyes. (have / has)
items.h: She = 3rd person singular → has got.
items.hd: She = 3. Person Einzahl → has got.
items.e: 'She' takes 'has': She has got blue eyes.
items.ed: 'She' nimmt 'has': She has got blue eyes.

items.p: He ___ got a sister. (have / has — negative)
items.h: He = 3rd person → negative = hasn't got.
items.hd: He = 3. Person → Verneinung = hasn't got.
items.e: He hasn't got a sister. 3rd person negative = hasn't.
items.ed: He hasn't got a sister. 3. Person Verneinung = hasn't.

items.p: ___ you got a pet? (have / has — question form)
items.h: Have you got...? / Has he got...? No 'do'!
items.hd: Have you got...? / Has he got...? Kein 'do'!
items.e: Questions: Have + I/you/we/they + got? Have you got a pet?
items.ed: Fragen: Have + I/you/we/they + got? Have you got a pet?

items.p: ___ she got a computer? — No, she ___. (question + short answer)
items.h: She = 3rd person → Has she got? — No, she hasn't.
items.hd: She = 3. Person → Has she got? — No, she hasn't.
items.e: Has she got? — No, she hasn't. (Not 'Does she has got?')
items.ed: Has she got? — No, she hasn't. (Nicht 'Does she has got?')

items.p: I ___ got any money. (negative)
items.h: I haven't got any... (NOT 'I haven't got no...')
items.hd: I haven't got any... (NICHT 'I haven't got no...')
items.e: I haven't got any money. Don't use double negation in English.
items.ed: I haven't got any money. Keine doppelte Verneinung auf Englisch.

items.p: Tom ___ a new bike.
items.h: Tom = he = 3rd person singular.
items.hd: Tom = he = 3. Person Einzahl.
items.e: Tom = he → has got: Tom has got a new bike.
items.ed: Tom = he → has got: Tom has got a new bike.

items.p: Which question is correct?
ds: Does he has got a dog?
items.h: Have-got questions: Have I/you/we/they got? Has he/she/it got?
items.hd: Have-got Fragen: Have I/you/we/they got? Has he/she/it got?
items.e: He = 3rd person → Has he got a dog? No 'do/does'!
items.ed: He = 3. Person → Has he got a dog? Kein 'do/does'!

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
ds: They don't have got any pets.
items.h: In negative sentences with 'have got', be careful not to use double negation.
items.hd: Bei verneinten Sätzen mit 'have got' — keine doppelte Verneinung!
items.e: They haven't got any pets. (Not 'no pets' with 'haven't'.)
items.ed: They haven't got any pets. (Nicht 'no pets' mit 'haven't'.)

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He have got a new phone.
items.c: He has got a new phone.
a: He has got a new phone.
he is got a new phone.
items.h: He = 3rd person. Have or has?
items.hd: He = 3. Person. Have oder has?
items.e: He = 3rd person → has: He has got a new phone.
items.ed: He = 3. Person → has: He has got a new phone.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Does he has got a cat?
items.h: Have-got questions don't use 'do/does'.
items.hd: Have-got Fragen verwenden kein 'do/does'.
items.e: Don't use do-support with 'have got': Has he got a cat?
items.ed: Kein do-support bei 'have got': Has he got a cat?

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I haven't got no friends here.
items.c: I haven't got any friends here.
a: I haven't got any friends here.
I have not got any friends here.
items.h: Is there a double negation?
items.hd: Gibt es eine doppelte Verneinung?
items.e: haven't + no = double negation. Use 'any' instead: haven't got any friends.
items.ed: haven't + no = doppelte Verneinung. Verwende 'any': haven't got any friends.

items.p: Your friend says Lisa has a cat, but that's wrong! Make this negative: She has got a cat. → She ___ got a cat.
items.h: has → hasn't (negative).
items.hd: has → hasn't (Verneinung).
items.e: She has got → She hasn't got a cat.
items.ed: She has got → She hasn't got a cat.

items.p: You want to ask your new classmate about pets. Make this a question: They have got two dogs. → ___ they ___ two dogs?
a: Have ... got they Have ... got two dogs?
Have ... got they Have ... got two dogs
Have ... got they Have ... got two dogs
items.h: Move 'have' to the front to make a question.
items.hd: Stelle 'have' nach vorne für eine Frage.
items.e: They have got → Have they got two dogs?
items.ed: They have got → Have they got two dogs?

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich habe einen Bruder.
items.h: 'Ich habe' = 'I have got'.
items.hd: 'Ich habe' = 'I have got'.
items.e: 'habe' = 'have got'. I have got a brother.
items.ed: 'habe' = 'have got'. I have got a brother.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Hat er einen Hund? — Nein, hat er nicht.
items.c: Has he got a dog? — No, he hasn't.
a: Has he got a dog? — No, he hasn't.
Has he got a dog? — No, he has not.
Has he got a dog? — No, he hasn't
items.h: 'Hat er' = 'Has he got'. Short answer: No, he hasn't.
items.hd: 'Hat er' = 'Has he got'. Kurzantwort: No, he hasn't.
items.e: 'Hat er einen Hund?' = 'Has he got a dog?' Short answer: No, he hasn't.
items.ed: 'Hat er einen Hund?' = 'Has he got a dog?' Kurzantwort: No, he hasn't.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: got / a / has / pen / he / ?
items.h: For questions, 'has' goes first.
items.hd: Für Fragen steht 'has' am Anfang.
items.e: Has + subject + got + object? Has he got a pen?
items.ed: Has + Subjekt + got + Objekt? Has he got a pen?

items.p: Match the subject to the correct form: 1) I 2) She 3) We 4) He — a) has got b) have got c) have got d) has got
items.h: I/you/we/they = have got. He/she/it = has got.
items.hd: I/you/we/they = have got. He/she/it = has got.
items.e: I have got, She has got, We have got, He has got.
items.ed: I have got, She has got, We have got, He has got.

items.p: This is ___ bag..
items.h: Add 's to show possession: Tom + 's = Tom's.
items.hd: Hänge 's an, um Besitz zu zeigen: Tom + 's = Tom's.
items.e: Tom's bag = the bag belonging to Tom. Use apostrophe + s.
items.ed: Tom's bag = die Tasche, die Tom gehört. Verwende Apostroph + s.

items.p: ___ name is Anna..
items.h: my sister + 's = my sister's (belonging to my sister).
items.hd: my sister + 's = my sister's (gehört meiner Schwester).
items.e: My sister's name = the name of my sister. Add 's.
items.ed: My sister's name = der Name meiner Schwester. Hänge 's an.

items.p: Where is ___ phone?.
items.h: Sarah + 's = Sarah's phone.
items.hd: Sarah + 's = Sarah's phone.
items.e: Sarah's phone. For singular names, always add 's (apostrophe + s).
items.ed: Sarah's phone. Bei einzelnen Namen immer 's anhängen (Apostroph + s).

items.p: My ___ birthday is in June..
items.h: mum + 's = mum's (belonging to mum).
items.hd: mum + 's = mum's (gehört meiner Mama).
items.e: My mum's birthday = the birthday of my mum.
items.ed: My mum's birthday = der Geburtstag meiner Mama.

items.p: ___ is nice. (meaning: Tom is nice — NOT possession!)
items.h: Tom's can mean 'Tom is' (contraction) or 'belonging to Tom'.
items.hd: Tom's kann 'Tom is' (Kurzform) oder 'gehört Tom' (Besitz) bedeuten.
items.e: Tom's = Tom is (here). Context tells you if it's possessive or contraction.
items.ed: Tom's = Tom is (hier). Der Kontext sagt dir, ob es Besitz oder Kurzform ist.

items.p: ___ cat is very cute..
items.h: Lisa + 's = Lisa's (the cat belongs to Lisa).
items.hd: Lisa + 's = Lisa's (die Katze gehört Lisa).
items.e: Lisa's cat. In English, use apostrophe + s (not just -s like German).
items.ed: Lisa's cat. Im Englischen Apostroph + s verwenden (nicht nur -s wie im Deutschen).

items.p: Which is correct? ___ is red.
items.h: Name + 's + thing owned.
items.hd: Name + 's + Besitz.
items.e: Anna's bag. Use apostrophe + s to show possession.
items.ed: Anna's bag. Verwende Apostroph + s für Besitz.

items.p: What does 'Tom's happy' mean?
ds: The happy thing belongs to Tom.
items.h: 'Tom's' can mean 'Tom is' or 'belonging to Tom'. Check the context!
items.hd: 'Tom's' kann 'Tom is' oder 'gehört Tom' bedeuten. Pruefe den Kontext!
items.e: Tom's happy = Tom is happy (contraction, not possession).
items.ed: Tom's happy = Tom is happy (Kurzform, nicht Besitz).

items.p: In which sentence does 's mean POSSESSION (not 'is')?
items.h: Which sentence talks about something belonging to someone?
items.hd: Welcher Satz spricht von etwas, das jemandem gehört?
items.e: My dad's car = the car of my dad (possession). The others use 's = is.
items.ed: My dad's car = das Auto meines Vaters (Besitz). Die anderen verwenden 's = is.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: This is Tom bag.
items.h: Something is missing between 'Tom' and 'bag'.
items.hd: Zwischen 'Tom' und 'bag' fehlt etwas.
items.e: You need apostrophe + s: Tom's bag.
items.ed: Du brauchst Apostroph + s: Tom's bag.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Marias cat is cute.
items.h: In English, the possessive needs an apostrophe.
items.hd: Im Englischen braucht der Genitiv einen Apostroph.
items.e: German: Marias Katze (no apostrophe). English: Maria's cat (with apostrophe).
items.ed: Deutsch: Marias Katze (kein Apostroph). Englisch: Maria's cat (mit Apostroph).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: is / nice / Tom's / bag
items.h: The word order is wrong. What comes after 'Tom's'?
items.hd: Die Wortstellung ist falsch. Was kommt nach 'Tom's'?
items.e: Tom's bag is nice. Possessive 's comes right before the noun it modifies.
items.ed: Tom's bag is nice. Das Besitz-'s steht direkt vor dem Nomen.
