items.ed: I → me als Objektpronomen. Help me (nicht 'help I').

items.p: Maria is nice. I like ___.
items.h: Maria = she. What is the object form of 'she'?
items.hd: Maria = she. Wie heisst die Objektform von 'she'?
items.e: She → her as object pronoun. I like her (not 'I like she').
items.ed: She → her als Objektpronomen. I like her (nicht 'I like she').

items.p: The present is for ___. (we)
items.h: After prepositions (for, to, with), use the object form.
items.hd: Nach Präpositionen (for, to, with) verwendet man die Objektform.
items.e: We → us as object pronoun. After prepositions, always use object pronouns: for us.
items.ed: We → us als Objektpronomen. Nach Präpositionen immer Objektpronomen: for us.

items.p: I see Tom and Lisa every day. I see ___ at school.
items.h: Tom and Lisa = they. What is the object form of 'they'?
items.hd: Tom und Lisa = they. Wie heisst die Objektform von 'they'?
items.e: They → them as object pronoun. I see them (not 'I see they').
items.ed: They → them als Objektpronomen. I see them (nicht 'I see they').

items.p: Tom sees Anna every day. He sees ___ at school.
items.h: Anna = she. What is the object pronoun for 'she'?
items.hd: Anna = she. Wie heisst das Objektpronomen für 'she'?
items.e: She → her (object). He sees her at school.
items.ed: She → her (Objekt). He sees her at school.

items.p: Please give the book to ___. (I)
items.h: After 'to', always use the object pronoun.
items.hd: Nach 'to' verwendet man immer das Objektpronomen.
items.e: After prepositions (to, for, with), use object pronouns: to me, for her, with them.
items.ed: Nach Präpositionen (to, for, with) verwende Objektpronomen: to me, for her, with them.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: Come with us to the park!
a: Come with us to the park!
ds: Come with we to the park!
Come with our to the park!
Come with they to the park!
items.h: After 'with', we need an object pronoun.
items.hd: Nach 'with' brauchen wir ein Objektpronomen.
items.e: We → us after prepositions: Come with us (not 'with we').
items.ed: We → us nach Präpositionen: Come with us (nicht 'with we').

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I like he very much.
items.h: After the verb 'like', we need an object pronoun, not a subject pronoun.
items.hd: Nach dem Verb 'like' brauchen wir ein Objektpronomen, kein Subjektpronomen.
items.e: He → him as object. I like him (not 'I like he').
items.ed: He → him als Objekt. I like him (nicht 'I like he').

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: This cake is for she.
items.h: After 'for', we need the object form.
items.hd: Nach 'for' brauchen wir die Objektform.
items.e: She → her after prepositions: for her (not 'for she').
items.ed: She → her nach Präpositionen: for her (nicht 'for she').

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Give the pencils to they, please.
items.c: Give the pencils to them, please.
a: Give the pencils to them, please.
items.h: After 'to', we need the object pronoun.
items.hd: Nach 'to' brauchen wir das Objektpronomen.
items.e: They → them after prepositions: to them (not 'to they').
items.ed: They → them nach Präpositionen: to them (nicht 'to they').

items.p: Replace the noun with a pronoun: I know the girls. → I know ___.
items.h: The girls = they. What is the object form?
items.hd: The girls = they. Wie heisst die Objektform?
items.e: The girls = they → object pronoun = them. I know them.
items.ed: The girls = they → Objektpronomen = them. I know them.

items.p: Replace the noun with a pronoun: Give the ball to my sister and me. → Give it to ___.
items.h: My sister and me = we. After 'to', use the object form.
items.hd: Meine Schwester und ich = we. Nach 'to' verwende die Objektform.
items.e: My sister and me = we → object = us. Give it to us.
items.ed: Meine Schwester und ich = we → Objekt = us. Give it to us.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Kannst du mir helfen?
items.h: 'mir' = me (object form of 'I').
items.hd: 'mir' = me (Objektform von 'I').
items.e: 'Mir' = 'me'. After a verb, use object pronouns: help me, see her, like him.
items.ed: 'Mir' = 'me'. Nach einem Verb verwende Objektpronomen: help me, see her, like him.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich mag sie (= Anna und Lisa). Ich sehe sie jeden Tag.
items.c: I like them. I see them every day.
a: I like them. I see them every day.
I like them. I see them every day
Every day i like them i see them
items.h: Anna und Lisa = they → object form = ?
items.hd: Anna und Lisa = they → Objektform = ?
items.e: 'Sie' (plural) = them. Be careful: German 'sie' can mean 'she', 'they', or 'you (formal)'!
items.ed: 'Sie' (Mehrzahl) = them. Vorsicht: Deutsch 'sie' kann 'she', 'they' oder 'Sie' (Hoeflichkeitsform) bedeuten!

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: me / help / please / Can / you / ?
items.h: Start with 'Can you...'.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'Can you...'.
items.e: Can you help me, please? Object pronoun 'me' goes after the verb.
items.ed: Can you help me, please? Das Objektpronomen 'me' steht nach dem Verb.

items.p: Match the subject pronoun to its object form: 1) I  2) he  3) she  4) they — a) them  b) me  c) her  d) him
items.h: Think: I→me, he→him, she→her, they→them.
items.hd: Denke: I→me, he→him, she→her, they→them.
items.e: I → me, he → him, she → her, they → them. Learn these pairs!
items.ed: I → me, he → him, she → her, they → them. Lerne diese Paare!

items.p: ___ is your name? — My name is Anna.
items.h: You are asking about a thing/name → 'What'.
items.hd: Du fragst nach einer Sache/einem Namen → 'What'.
items.e: What = thing/name: What is your name? What do you like?
items.ed: What = Sache/Name: What is your name? What do you like?

items.p: ___ do you live? — I live in Vienna.
items.h: The answer is a place (Vienna) → which question word?
items.hd: Die Antwort ist ein Ort (Wien) → welches Fragewort?
items.e: Where = place: Where do you live? Where is the school?
items.ed: Where = Ort: Where do you live? Where is the school?

items.p: ___ is your best friend? — Tom is my best friend.
items.h: The answer is a person (Tom) → which question word?
items.hd: Die Antwort ist eine Person (Tom) → welches Fragewort?
items.e: Who = person: Who is your friend? Who likes pizza?
items.ed: Who = Person: Who is your friend? Who likes pizza?

items.p: ___ do you feed your cat? — Twice a day.
items.h: The answer is about frequency (twice a day) → which question words?
items.hd: Die Antwort ist über Häufigkeit (zweimal am Tag) → welche Fragewörter?
items.e: How often = frequency: How often do you feed your cat? Twice a day.
items.ed: How often = Häufigkeit: How often do you feed your cat? Twice a day.

items.p: ___ are you sad? — Because my dog is sick.
items.h: The answer gives a reason (because...) → which question word?
items.hd: Die Antwort gibt einen Grund (because...) → welches Fragewort?
items.e: Why = reason: Why are you sad? Because my dog is sick.
items.ed: Why = Grund: Why are you sad? Because my dog is sick.

items.p: ___ do you eat for breakfast? — I eat cereal.
items.h: The answer is a thing (cereal) → which question word?
items.hd: Die Antwort ist eine Sache (Cerealien) → welches Fragewort?
items.e: What = thing: What do you eat? What is this?
items.ed: What = Sache: What do you eat? What is this?

items.p: Which question is correct?
items.h: Question word + does + subject + base verb?
items.hd: Fragewort + does + Subjekt + Grundform?
items.e: Wh-question: Where + does + she + live? (base form, no -s!)
items.ed: W-Frage: Where + does + she + live? (Grundform, kein -s!)

items.p: Choose the correct question with 'who' as the subject:
items.c: Who speaks French in your class?
a: Who speaks French in your class?
ds: Who does speak French in your class?
Who do speak French in your class?
Who speak French in your class?
items.h: When 'who' is the SUBJECT of the question, don't use do/does!
items.hd: Wenn 'who' das SUBJEKT der Frage ist, kein do/does verwenden!
items.e: Who (as subject) + verb-s: Who speaks French? No 'does' needed because 'who' replaces the subject.
items.ed: Who (als Subjekt) + Verb-s: Who speaks French? Kein 'does' nötig, weil 'who' das Subjekt ersetzt.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Where you live?
items.h: English wh-questions need 'do' or 'does' after the question word.
items.hd: Englische W-Fragen brauchen 'do' oder 'does' nach dem Fragewort.
items.e: Where do you live? English needs 'do/does' in present simple questions (unlike German).
items.ed: Where do you live? Englisch braucht 'do/does' in Present-Simple-Fragen (anders als Deutsch).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: What does she likes?
items.h: After 'does', the verb stays in base form — no -s!
items.hd: Nach 'does' bleibt das Verb in der Grundform — kein -s!
items.e: What does she like? (not likes). The -s is already in 'does'.
items.ed: What does she like? (nicht likes). Das -s steckt schon in 'does'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Where is your name?
items.h: Is a name a place or a thing?
items.hd: Ist ein Name ein Ort oder eine Sache?
items.e: 'Where' = place. 'What' = thing/name. A name is a thing, not a place!
items.ed: 'Where' = Ort. 'What' = Sache/Name. Ein Name ist eine Sache, kein Ort!

items.p: Write a question for this answer: 'I live in Salzburg.' → ___ do you live?
items.h: The answer is about a place.
items.hd: Die Antwort handelt von einem Ort.
items.e: The answer is a place (Salzburg), so the question word is 'Where'.
items.ed: Die Antwort ist ein Ort (Salzburg), also ist das Fragewort 'Where'.

items.p: Write a question for this answer: 'I feed my dog three times a day.' → ___ ___ do you feed your dog?
a: How often How often do you feed your dog?
How often How often do you feed your dog
items.h: The answer tells you about frequency (three times a day).
items.hd: Die Antwort gibt Auskunft über die Häufigkeit (dreimal am Tag).
items.e: 'Three times a day' is a frequency expression → question word = 'How often'.
items.ed: 'Three times a day' ist ein Häufigkeitsausdruck → Fragewort = 'How often'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wo wohnst du?
items.h: 'Wo' = Where. Don't forget 'do'!
items.hd: 'Wo' = Where. Vergiss nicht 'do'!
items.e: 'Wo' = 'Where'. English needs 'do': Where do you live? (not 'Where you live?').
items.ed: 'Wo' = 'Where'. Englisch braucht 'do': Where do you live? (nicht 'Where you live?').

items.p: 🇩🇪 Warum magst du Katzen?
items.h: 'Warum' = Why. Remember: Question word + do + subject + base verb?
items.hd: 'Warum' = Why. Merke: Fragewort + do + Subjekt + Grundform?
items.e: 'Warum' = 'Why'. Why + do + you + like? Full structure with do-support.
items.ed: 'Warum' = 'Why'. Why + do + you + like? Volle Struktur mit do-Unterstuetzung.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: does / What / like / she / ?
items.h: Question word first, then does, then subject, then verb.
items.hd: Fragewort zuerst, dann does, dann Subjekt, dann Verb.
items.e: What does she like? Word order: QW + does/do + subject + base verb?
items.ed: What does she like? Wortstellung: Fragewort + does/do + Subjekt + Grundform?

items.p: Match the question word to the answer type: 1) Who  2) What  3) Where  4) Why — a) a reason  b) a place  c) a person  d) a thing
items.h: Who = person, What = thing, Where = place, Why = reason.
items.hd: Who = Person, What = Sache, Where = Ort, Why = Grund.
items.e: Who → person. What → thing. Where → place. Why → reason.
items.ed: Who → Person. What → Sache. Where → Ort. Why → Grund.

items.p: Tom lives in London. Ask about the place: ___ does Tom live?
items.h: You want to know the PLACE → Where.
items.hd: Du willst den ORT wissen → Where.
items.e: Where does Tom live? (not 'Where does Tom lives?' — no -s after does!)
items.ed: Where does Tom live? (nicht 'Where does Tom lives?' — kein -s nach does!)

items.p: I brush my teeth ___ a day. (2x)
items.h: 1x = once, 2x = twice.
items.hd: 1x = once, 2x = twice.
items.e: 'Twice' is the English word for '2 times'. Once (1x), twice (2x), three times (3x).
items.ed: 'Twice' ist das englische Wort für 'zweimal'. Once (1x), twice (2x), three times (3x).

items.p: We have English ___ a week. (3x)
items.h: From 3 onwards: number + times. Three ___.
items.hd: Ab 3: Zahl + times. Three ___.
items.e: Once, twice, then: three times, four times, five times... Always 'times' (plural) from 3+.
items.ed: Once, twice, dann: three times, four times, five times... Ab 3 immer 'times' (Mehrzahl).

items.p: She goes swimming ___ a week. (1x)
items.h: 1x = once (not 'one time').
items.hd: 1x = once (nicht 'one time').
items.e: 'Once' means 'one time'. It's a special word — don't say 'one time a week'.
items.ed: 'Once' bedeutet 'einmal'. Es ist ein besonderes Wort — sag nicht 'one time a week'.

items.p: He plays football ___ day..
items.h: 'Every' + singular noun (no article!): every day, every week.
items.hd: 'Every' + Nomen in der Einzahl (kein Artikel!): every day, every week.
items.e: 'Every day' = 'jeden Tag'. 'Every' + singular noun, never 'every the day'.
items.ed: 'Every day' = 'jeden Tag'. 'Every' + Einzahlnomen, nie 'every the day'.

items.p: I feed my hamster ___ a day and clean its cage ___ a week.
items.h: 1x = once, 2x = twice. These are special English words.
items.hd: 1x = once, 2x = twice. Das sind besondere englische Wörter.
items.e: Twice a day (not 'two times'). Once a week (not 'one time'). English uses special words for 1x and 2x.
items.ed: Twice a day (nicht 'two times'). Once a week (nicht 'one time'). Englisch hat eigene Wörter für 1x und 2x.

items.p: I go swimming ___ a week.
items.h: 2x a week = ? a week (special English word).
items.hd: 2x pro Woche = ? a week (spezielles englisches Wort).
items.e: 'Twice' = two times. Use 'twice' (not 'two times') in standard English.
items.ed: 'Twice' = zweimal. Verwende 'twice' (nicht 'two times') im Standardenglisch.

items.p: Which sentence has the correct word order?
items.c: She feeds the cat twice a day.
a: She feeds the cat twice a day.
ds: She twice a day feeds the cat.
She feeds twice a day the cat.
Twice a day she feeds the cat she.
items.h: Frequency expressions usually go at the END of the sentence.
items.hd: Häufigkeitsausdrücke stehen normalerweise am ENDE des Satzes.
items.e: She feeds the cat twice a day. Frequency expressions (once/twice/three times a day) go at the end.
items.ed: She feeds the cat twice a day. Häufigkeitsausdrücke (once/twice/three times a day) stehen am Ende.

items.p: Which expression is correct?
items.h: The pattern is: once/twice + a + singular noun (day/week/month/year).
items.hd: Das Muster ist: once/twice + a + Einzahlnomen (day/week/month/year).
items.e: Once a month (not 'one time a month'). Pattern: once/twice + a + singular time word.
items.ed: Once a month (nicht 'one time a month'). Muster: once/twice + a + Zeitwort in der Einzahl.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I go swimming one time a month.
items.c: I go swimming once a month.
a: I go swimming once a month.
items.h: What is the shorter English word for 'one time'?
items.hd: Was ist das kürzere englische Wort für 'one time'?
items.e: 'Twice' = two times. Use 'twice' in standard English.
items.ed: 'Twice' = zweimal. Verwende 'twice' im Standardenglisch.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I go swimming one time a week.
items.c: I go swimming once a week.
a: I go swimming once a week.
items.h: What is the shorter English word for 'one time'?
items.hd: Was ist das kürzere englische Wort für 'one time'?
items.e: 'One time' = 'once'. Frequency expressions go at the END: I go swimming once a week.
items.ed: 'One time' = 'once'. Häufigkeitsausdrücke stehen am ENDE: I go swimming once a week.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She plays tennis three time a week.
items.c: She plays tennis three times a week.
a: She plays tennis three times a week.
items.h: From 3 onwards, 'times' needs a plural -s.
items.hd: Ab 3 braucht 'times' ein Plural-s.
items.e: Three times (not 'three time'). 'Times' must be plural for 3+.
items.ed: Three times (nicht 'three time'). 'Times' muss ab 3 im Plural stehen.

items.p: Write the frequency expression: 2 x week → ___ a ___.
a: twice a week a twice a week.
twice a week a twice a week
items.h: 2x = twice. Then: a + time word.
items.hd: 2x = twice. Dann: a + Zeitwort.
items.e: 2 x week = twice a week. Pattern: once/twice + a + time word.
items.ed: 2 x Woche = twice a week. Muster: once/twice + a + Zeitwort.

items.p: Your friend visits her grandma regularly. Describe how often. Write the frequency expression: 4 x month → ___ ___ a ___.
a: four times a month four times a month a four times a month.
four times a month four times a month a four times a month
items.h: From 3+: number + times + a + time word.
items.hd: Ab 3: Zahl + times + a + Zeitwort.
items.e: 4 x month = four times a month. Pattern: number + times + a + time word.
items.ed: 4 x Monat = four times a month. Muster: Zahl + times + a + Zeitwort.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich putze mir zweimal am Tag die Zähne.
items.c: I brush my teeth twice a day.
a: I brush my teeth twice a day.
items.h: 'zweimal' = twice. 'am Tag' = a day.
items.hd: 'zweimal' = twice. 'am Tag' = a day.
items.e: 'Zweimal am Tag' = 'twice a day'. Frequency expression goes at the end.
items.ed: 'Zweimal am Tag' = 'twice a day'. Der Häufigkeitsausdruck steht am Ende.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wir haben dreimal pro Woche Englisch.
items.c: We have English three times a week.
a: We have English three times a week.
we've English three times a week.
items.h: 'dreimal pro Woche' = three times a week.
items.hd: 'dreimal pro Woche' = three times a week.
items.e: 'Dreimal pro Woche' = 'three times a week'. Pattern: number + times + a + time word.
items.ed: 'Dreimal pro Woche' = 'three times a week'. Muster: Zahl + times + a + Zeitwort.

items.p: I walk my dog ___ a day.
items.h: Use different frequency expressions in each sentence.
items.hd: Verwende in jedem Satz einen anderen Häufigkeitsausdruck.
items.e: Frequency expressions: once a day, twice a day, three times a week, every day, every week.
items.ed: Häufigkeitsausdrücke: once a day, twice a day, three times a week, every day, every week.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: a / my / I / twice / teeth / day / brush
items.c: I brush my teeth twice a day.
a: I brush my teeth twice a day.
items.h: Subject + verb + object + frequency expression.
items.hd: Subjekt + Verb + Objekt + Häufigkeitsausdruck.
items.e: I brush my teeth twice a day. Frequency expressions go at the end.
items.ed: I brush my teeth twice a day. Häufigkeitsausdrücke stehen am Ende.

items.p: Match the expression to its meaning: 1) once a day  2) twice a week  3) three times a month  4) every day — a) 3x pro Monat  b) 1x pro Tag  c) jeden Tag  d) 2x pro Woche
items.h: once = einmal, twice = zweimal, three times = dreimal, every = jede(n).
items.hd: once = einmal, twice = zweimal, three times = dreimal, every = jede(n).
items.e: once a day = 1x pro Tag, twice a week = 2x pro Woche, three times a month = 3x pro Monat, every day = jeden Tag.
items.ed: once a day = 1x pro Tag, twice a week = 2x pro Woche, three times a month = 3x pro Monat, every day = jeden Tag.

items.p: My parents are great. I love ___ and they love ___.
items.h: After 'love' (a verb), use object pronouns: me, him, her, us, them.
items.hd: Nach 'love' (einem Verb) verwende Objektpronomen: me, him, her, us, them.
items.e: I love them (not 'they'). They love me (not 'I'). Object pronouns come after verbs.
items.ed: I love them (nicht 'they'). They love me (nicht 'I'). Objektpronomen stehen nach Verben.

items.p: I practise the piano ___ day and I have a piano lesson ___ a week.
items.h: 'Every day' = each day. 'Once a week' = 1x per week.
items.hd: 'Every day' = jeden Tag. 'Once a week' = 1x pro Woche.
items.e: 'Every' + singular noun = each one. 'Once a week' = one time per week.
items.ed: 'Every' + Einzahlnomen = jede(n). 'Once a week' = einmal pro Woche.

items.p: _____ is my pencil. (It's here in my hand.)
items.h: The pencil is near you (here). Is it one pencil or many?
items.hd: Der Bleistift ist bei dir (hier). Ist es ein Bleistift oder viele?
items.e: Use 'this' for one thing that is near you.
items.ed: Verwende 'this' für eine Sache, die in deiner Nähe ist.

items.p: _____ are my books. (They're here on my desk.)
items.h: The books are near you. Is it one book or many?
items.hd: Die Bücher sind bei dir. Ist es ein Buch oder viele?
items.e: Use 'these' for more than one thing that is near you.
items.ed: Verwende 'these' für mehrere Dinge, die in deiner Nähe sind.

items.p: Look at _____ bird over there in the tree!
items.h: The bird is far away (over there). Is it one bird or many?
items.hd: Der Vogel ist weit weg (dort drüben). Ist es ein Vogel oder viele?
items.e: 'That' is for one thing that is far from you.
items.ed: 'That' wird für eine Sache verwendet, die weit weg ist.

items.p: _____ shoes over there are really cool!
items.h: The shoes are far away (over there). Is it one shoe or many?
items.hd: Die Schuhe sind weit weg (dort drüben). Ist es ein Schuh oder viele?
items.e: Use 'those' for more than one thing that is far from you.
items.ed: Verwende 'those' für mehrere Dinge, die weit weg sind.

items.p: I like _____ T-shirt here, but I don't like _____ sweater over there.
items.h: The T-shirt is near (here). The sweater is far (over there). Both are singular.
items.hd: Das T-Shirt ist nah (hier). Der Pullover ist weit weg (dort drüben). Beide sind Einzahl.
items.e: Use 'this' for near + singular and 'that' for far + singular.
items.ed: Verwende 'this' für nah + Einzahl und 'that' für weit weg + Einzahl.

items.p: Are _____ your keys here on the table?
items.h: The keys are near (here on the table). Is it one key or many?
items.hd: Die Schlüssel sind nah (hier auf dem Tisch). Ist es ein Schlüssel oder viele?
items.e: 'These' is for plural things near you. Keys are plural and they're here.
items.ed: 'These' wird für mehrere Dinge in der Nähe verwendet. Schlüssel (keys) ist Mehrzahl und sie sind hier.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: Those dogs over there are very loud.
a: Those dogs over there are very loud.
ds: That dogs over there are very loud.
This dogs over there are very loud.
These dogs over there are very loud.
items.h: The dogs are far away (over there) and they are plural (dogs, not dog).
items.hd: Die Hunde sind weit weg (dort drüben) und es ist Mehrzahl (dogs, nicht dog).
items.e: 'Those' is the correct demonstrative for plural + far. 'That' is far but singular. 'These' is plural but near.
items.ed: 'Those' ist richtig für Mehrzahl + weit weg. 'That' ist weit weg aber Einzahl. 'These' ist Mehrzahl aber nah.

items.p: Choose the correct option: '_____ flowers in the garden are beautiful.'
items.h: The flowers are in the garden (not right next to you). Are they singular or plural?
items.hd: Die Blumen sind im Garten (nicht direkt neben dir). Sind sie Einzahl oder Mehrzahl?
items.e: 'Flowers' is plural and they are in the garden (far), so we use 'those'.
items.ed: 'Flowers' ist Mehrzahl und sie sind im Garten (weit weg), daher verwenden wir 'those'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: This shoes are really nice.
items.h: Look at the noun 'shoes'. Is it singular or plural?
items.hd: Schau dir das Nomen 'shoes' an. Ist es Einzahl oder Mehrzahl?
items.e: 'Shoes' is plural, so we need the plural demonstrative 'these', not the singular 'this'.
items.ed: 'Shoes' ist Mehrzahl, daher brauchen wir das Mehrzahl-Demonstrativpronomen 'these', nicht das Einzahl-'this'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: That books over there are interesting.
items.c: Those books over there are interesting.
a: Those books over there are interesting.
items.h: 'Books' is plural. Which demonstrative do we need for plural + far?
items.hd: 'Books' ist Mehrzahl. Welches Demonstrativpronomen brauchen wir für Mehrzahl + weit weg?
items.e: 'Books' is plural and 'over there' means far, so we need 'those', not 'that'.
items.ed: 'Books' ist Mehrzahl und 'over there' bedeutet weit weg, also brauchen wir 'those', nicht 'that'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: These computer here is very fast.
items.c: This computer here is very fast.
a: This computer here is very fast.
items.h: 'Computer' is singular. Which demonstrative matches a singular near object?
items.hd: 'Computer' ist Einzahl. Welches Demonstrativpronomen passt zu einem einzelnen nahen Objekt?
items.e: 'Computer' is singular and 'here' means near, so use 'this', not 'these'.
items.ed: 'Computer' ist Einzahl und 'here' bedeutet nah, also verwende 'this', nicht 'these'.

items.p: Change from near to far: This pen is blue. → _____ pen over there is blue.
a: That pen over there is blue.
That pen over there is blue
items.h: When something moves from near to far, 'this' becomes …?
items.hd: Wenn etwas von nah nach weit weg wechselt, wird 'this' zu …?
items.e: 'This' (near, singular) becomes 'that' (far, singular) when the object is far away.
items.ed: 'This' (nah, Einzahl) wird zu 'that' (weit weg, Einzahl), wenn das Objekt weit weg ist.

items.p: Change from singular to plural: This apple is green. → _____ apples are green.
items.h: When one thing becomes many things, 'this' becomes …?
items.hd: Wenn eine Sache zu vielen wird, wird 'this' zu …?
items.e: 'This' (singular, near) becomes 'these' (plural, near) when the noun is plural.
items.ed: 'This' (Einzahl, nah) wird zu 'these' (Mehrzahl, nah), wenn das Nomen in der Mehrzahl steht.

items.p: Translate into English: Das ist mein Hund. (The dog is near you.)
items.h: The dog is near you → use 'this'. 'Mein' = my.
items.hd: Der Hund ist nah bei dir → verwende 'this'. 'Mein' = my.
items.e: German 'Das' can mean this/that. Since the dog is near, we use 'this'.
items.ed: Deutsch 'Das' kann this oder that bedeuten. Da der Hund nah ist, verwenden wir 'this'.

items.p: Translate into English: Das sind meine Buecher dort drueben.
items.c: Those are my books over there.
a: Those are my books over there.
Those are my books over there
items.h: 'Dort drueben' = over there (far). 'Buecher' = books (plural).
items.hd: 'Dort drüben' = over there (weit weg). 'Bücher' = books (Mehrzahl).
items.e: Far + plural = 'those'. German 'Das sind' becomes 'Those are' when referring to far, plural objects.
items.ed: Weit weg + Mehrzahl = 'those'. Deutsch 'Das sind' wird zu 'Those are', wenn es um entfernte Objekte in der Mehrzahl geht.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: are / these / favourite / my / shoes
items.h: Start with the demonstrative pronoun. Then the verb.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Demonstrativpronomen. Dann das Verb.
items.e: The correct order is: These (subject) + are (verb) + my favourite shoes (complement).
items.ed: Die richtige Reihenfolge ist: These (Subjekt) + are (Verb) + my favourite shoes (Ergaenzung).

items.p: Match the demonstrative with the correct situation:
ds: 1: one thing, near you|2: many things, near you|3: one thing, far away|4: many things, far away
items.h: Think about near/far and singular/plural.
items.hd: Denke an nah/weit weg und Einzahl/Mehrzahl.
items.e: this = near + singular, these = near + plural, that = far + singular, those = far + plural.
items.ed: this = nah + Einzahl, these = nah + Mehrzahl, that = weit weg + Einzahl, those = weit weg + Mehrzahl.

items.p: ___ trainers over there are really cool!
items.h: 'Trainers' is plural and 'over there' means far away.
items.hd: 'Trainers' ist Mehrzahl und 'over there' bedeutet weit weg.
items.e: Far + plural = 'those'. Trainers are shoes (plural) and they're over there (far).
items.ed: Weit weg + Mehrzahl = 'those'. Trainers sind Schuhe (Mehrzahl) und sie sind dort drüben (weit weg).

items.p: Can I have ___ apple here, please?
items.h: 'Sandwich' is singular and 'here' means near.
items.hd: 'Sandwich' ist Einzahl und 'here' bedeutet nah.
items.e: Near + singular = 'this'. The sandwich is here (near) and it's just one (singular).
items.ed: Nah + Einzahl = 'this'. Das Sandwich ist hier (nah) und es ist nur eines (Einzahl).

items.p: How much _____ the bag?
items.h: 'Bag' is singular. We use 'How much + is/are'.
items.hd: 'Bag' ist Einzahl. Wir verwenden 'How much + is/are'.
items.e: 'Bag' is singular, so we use 'is'. How much is the bag?
items.ed: 'Bag' ist Einzahl, also verwenden wir 'is'. How much is the bag?

items.p: How much _____ the trainers?
items.h: 'Trainers' is plural. We use 'How much + is/are'.
items.hd: 'Trainers' ist Mehrzahl. Wir verwenden 'How much + is/are'.
items.e: 'Trainers' is plural, so we use 'are'. How much are the trainers?
items.ed: 'Trainers' ist Mehrzahl, also verwenden wir 'are'. How much are the trainers?

items.p: How much _____ this computer game?
items.h: 'Computer game' is one thing (singular).
items.hd: 'Computer game' ist eine Sache (Einzahl).
items.e: 'Computer game' is singular, so we use 'is': How much is this computer game?
items.ed: 'Computer game' ist Einzahl, also verwenden wir 'is': How much is this computer game?

items.p: How much _____ those jeans?
items.h: 'Jeans' is always plural in English.
items.hd: 'Jeans' ist im Englischen immer Mehrzahl.
items.e: 'Jeans' is plural (like trousers, shorts), so we use 'are': How much are those jeans?
items.ed: 'Jeans' ist Mehrzahl (wie trousers, shorts), also verwenden wir 'are': How much are those jeans?

items.p: _____ _____ is the T-shirt? – It's fifteen euros.
items.h: To ask about a price, we use 'How much + is/are'.
items.hd: Um nach einem Preis zu fragen, verwenden wir 'How much + is/are'.
items.e: We use 'How much' to ask about prices. 'How many' asks about countable numbers.
items.ed: Wir verwenden 'How much', um nach Preisen zu fragen. 'How many' fragt nach zaehlbaren Mengen.

items.p: Which question is correct?
items.h: Look at the noun — is it singular or plural? Choose the matching verb.
items.hd: Schau dir das Nomen an — ist es Einzahl oder Mehrzahl?
items.e: 'Shoes' is plural → 'are'. In English, we say 'How much is/are ...?', not 'How much costs ...?'.
items.ed: 'Shoes' ist Mehrzahl → 'are'. Im Englischen sagen wir 'How much is/are ...?', nicht 'How much costs ...?'.

items.p: Which answer is correct? 'How much is the hat?' – '_____ ten euros.'
items.h: 'Hat' is singular, so the answer starts with 'It...'
items.hd: 'Hat' ist Einzahl, also beginnt die Antwort mit 'It...'
items.e: For singular items, the answer is 'It's + price'. How much is the hat? – It's ten euros.
items.ed: Für einzelne Dinge ist die Antwort 'It's + Preis'. How much is the hat? – It's ten euros.

items.p: Which answer is correct? 'How much are the sunglasses?' – '_____ twenty-five euros.'
items.h: 'Sunglasses' is plural, so the answer starts with 'They...'
items.hd: 'Sunglasses' ist Mehrzahl, also beginnt die Antwort mit 'They...'
items.e: For plural items, the answer is 'They're + price'. How much are the sunglasses? – They're twenty-five euros.
items.ed: Für mehrere Dinge ist die Antwort 'They're + Preis'. How much are the sunglasses? – They're twenty-five euros.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: How much costs it?
items.h: In English, we don't use 'costs' in this question. We use 'is' or 'are'.
items.hd: Im Englischen verwenden wir nicht 'costs' in dieser Frage. Wir verwenden 'is' oder 'are'.
items.e: In English, we ask 'How much is it?', not 'How much costs it?' That's a German pattern (Wie viel kostet es?).
items.ed: Im Englischen fragen wir 'How much is it?', nicht 'How much costs it?' Das ist ein deutsches Muster (Wie viel kostet es?).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: How much is the bananas?
items.h: 'Bananas' is plural. Which verb form goes with plural nouns?
items.hd: 'Bananas' ist Mehrzahl. Welche Verbform passt zur Mehrzahl?
items.e: 'Bananas' is plural, so we need 'are', not 'is': How much are the bananas?
items.ed: 'Bananas' ist Mehrzahl, also brauchen wir 'are', nicht 'is': How much are the bananas?

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: How many is the book?
items.h: To ask about price, do we use 'how many' or 'how much'?
items.hd: Um nach dem Preis zu fragen, verwenden wir 'how many' oder 'how much'?
items.e: We use 'How much' for prices, not 'How many'. 'How many' asks about countable numbers (How many books?).
items.ed: Wir verwenden 'How much' für Preise, nicht 'How many'. 'How many' fragt nach zaehlbaren Mengen (How many books?).

items.p: Change the noun from singular to plural and adjust the verb: How much is the apple? → How much ___ the _____?
items.h: When the noun becomes plural, the verb changes too.
items.hd: Wenn das Nomen in die Mehrzahl wechselt, ändert sich auch das Verb.
items.e: When the noun is plural (apples), the verb must also be plural: 'are' instead of 'is'.
items.ed: Wenn das Nomen in der Mehrzahl steht (apples), muss auch das Verb in der Mehrzahl stehen: 'are' statt 'is'.

items.p: The pencil case costs twelve euros. Ask about the price.
items.c: How much is the pencil case?
a: How much is the pencil case?
How much is this pencil case?
items.h: Use 'How much is/are ...?' to ask about a price.
items.hd: Verwende 'How much is/are ...?', um nach dem Preis zu fragen.
items.e: To ask about the price of a singular item, use 'How much is + noun?'
items.ed: Um nach dem Preis eines einzelnen Artikels zu fragen, verwende 'How much is + Nomen?'

items.p: The sunglasses cost thirty euros. Ask about the price.
items.h: 'Sunglasses' is plural. Use the correct form of 'be'.
items.hd: 'Sunglasses' ist Mehrzahl. Verwende die richtige Form von 'be'.
items.e: 'Sunglasses' is plural → 'How much are the sunglasses?'
items.ed: 'Sunglasses' ist Mehrzahl → 'How much are the sunglasses?'

items.p: Translate into English: Wie viel kostet das T-Shirt?
items.h: In English, we don't use 'costs'. We use 'How much is/are ...'.
items.hd: Im Englischen verwenden wir nicht 'costs'. Wir verwenden 'How much is/are ...'.
items.e: German 'Wie viel kostet ...?' = English 'How much is ...?' (not 'How much costs').
items.ed: Deutsch 'Wie viel kostet ...?' = Englisch 'How much is ...?' (nicht 'How much costs').

items.p: Translate into English: Wie viel kosten die Schuhe? – Sie kosten dreissig Euro.
items.c: How much are the shoes? – They're thirty euros.
a: How much are the shoes? – They're thirty euros.
How much are the shoes? - They're thirty euros.
How much are the shoes? They're thirty euros.
How much are the shoes? – They are thirty euros.
How much are the shoes? – They're thirty euros
How much are the shoes? - they are thirty euros.
How much are the shoes? they are thirty euros.
items.h: 'Schuhe' = shoes (plural). Careful: don't use 'costs' in English!
items.hd: 'Schuhe' = shoes (Mehrzahl). Achtung: Verwende nicht 'costs' im Englischen!
items.e: German 'Wie viel kosten ...' = 'How much are ...' and 'Sie kosten ...' = 'They're ...'
items.ed: Deutsch 'Wie viel kosten ...' = 'How much are ...' und 'Sie kosten ...' = 'They're ...'

items.p: How much ___ the jacket?
items.h: 'Jacket' is singular.
items.hd: 'Jacket' ist Einzahl.
items.e: 'Jacket' is singular → 'How much is the jacket?'
items.ed: 'Jacket' ist Einzahl → 'How much is the jacket?'

items.p: How much ___ the headphones?
items.h: 'Headphones' is plural, so the answer starts with 'They...'
items.hd: 'Headphones' ist Mehrzahl, also beginnt die Antwort mit 'They...'
items.e: Plural → 'They're + price'. How much are the headphones? – They're twenty euros.
items.ed: Mehrzahl → 'They're + Preis'. How much are the headphones? – They're twenty euros.

items.p: Look! She _____ (play) tennis right now.
items.h: For actions happening NOW, use: am/is/are + verb-ing.
items.hd: Für Handlungen, die JETZT passieren: am/is/are + Verb-ing.
items.e: 'She' + 'is' + 'playing' = She is playing. We use present continuous for actions happening right now.
items.ed: 'She' + 'is' + 'playing' = She is playing. Wir verwenden das Present Continuous für Handlungen, die gerade passieren.

items.p: I _____ (read) a book at the moment.
items.h: Use 'I am + verb-ing' for things happening now.
items.hd: Verwende 'I am + Verb-ing' für Dinge, die jetzt passieren.
items.e: 'I' always goes with 'am': I am reading. 'At the moment' signals present continuous.
items.ed: 'I' geht immer mit 'am': I am reading. 'At the moment' zeigt das Present Continuous an.

items.p: They _____ (watch) TV right now.
items.h: 'They' goes with 'are'. Don't forget: am/is/are + verb-ing.
items.hd: 'They' geht mit 'are'. Nicht vergessen: am/is/are + Verb-ing.
items.e: 'They' + 'are' + 'watching'. They are watching TV right now.
items.ed: 'They' + 'are' + 'watching'. They are watching TV right now.

items.p: He _____ (ride) his bike to school today.
items.h: Remember the spelling rule: drop the -e before adding -ing (ride → riding).
items.hd: Denke an die Rechtschreibregel: Das -e faellt weg vor -ing (ride → riding).
items.e: ride → riding (drop the -e). He is riding his bike.
items.ed: ride → riding (das -e faellt weg). He is riding his bike.

items.p: The children _____ (swim) in the pool right now.
items.h: swim → swimming (double the -m). 'Children' is plural → are.
items.hd: swim → swimming (das -m wird verdoppelt). 'Children' ist Mehrzahl → are.
items.e: swim → swimming (double consonant after short vowel). Children are swimming.
items.ed: swim → swimming (Konsonantenverdopplung nach kurzem Vokal). Children are swimming.

items.p: She _____ (not/listen) to music right now.
items.h: Negative present continuous: am not / isn't / aren't + verb-ing.
items.hd: Verneintes Present Continuous: am not / isn't / aren't + Verb-ing.
items.e: For negative present continuous, use: subject + isn't/aren't + verb-ing. She isn't listening.
items.ed: Für das verneinte Present Continuous: Subjekt + isn't/aren't + Verb-ing. She isn't listening.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.h: Remember: drop the -e before -ing (make → making). Don't forget 'is'!
items.hd: Denke daran: Das -e faellt weg vor -ing (make → making). Vergiss 'is' nicht!
items.e: make → making (drop -e). Present continuous = be + verb-ing: She is making a cake.
items.ed: make → making (-e faellt weg). Present Continuous = be + Verb-ing: She is making a cake.

items.p: Choose the correct question:
items.c: Are you coming to the party?
a: Are you coming to the party?
ds: Do you coming to the party?
You are coming to the party?
Is you coming to the party?
items.h: For present continuous questions, put 'am/is/are' BEFORE the subject.
items.hd: Für Fragen im Present Continuous kommt 'am/is/are' VOR das Subjekt.
items.e: Present continuous questions: Am/Is/Are + subject + verb-ing? Are you coming?
items.ed: Fragen im Present Continuous: Am/Is/Are + Subjekt + Verb-ing? Are you coming?

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She playing football right now.
items.c: She is playing football right now.
a: She is playing football right now.
items.h: Something is missing between 'She' and 'playing'. What do you need for present continuous?
items.hd: Zwischen 'She' und 'playing' fehlt etwas. Was braucht man für das Present Continuous?
items.e: Present continuous needs the 'be' auxiliary: She IS playing. German has no continuous form, so it's easy to forget 'is/are'.
items.ed: Das Present Continuous braucht das Hilfsverb 'be': She IS playing. Im Deutschen gibt es keine Verlaufsform, daher vergisst man leicht 'is/are'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Do you playing computer games?
items.h: Present continuous questions don't use 'do'. They use 'am/is/are'.
items.hd: Fragen im Present Continuous verwenden nicht 'do'. Sie verwenden 'am/is/are'.
items.e: For present continuous questions, use: Are you playing? (not: Do you playing?). 'Do' is for present simple.
items.ed: Für Fragen im Present Continuous: Are you playing? (nicht: Do you playing?). 'Do' ist für das Present Simple.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She doesn't reading a book.
a: She is not reading a book.
items.h: The negative of present continuous doesn't use 'doesn't'. It uses 'isn't/aren't'.
items.hd: Die Verneinung des Present Continuous verwendet nicht 'doesn't'. Sie verwendet 'isn't/aren't'.
items.e: For negative present continuous: She isn't reading. 'Doesn't' is for present simple negative.
items.ed: Für die Verneinung im Present Continuous: She isn't reading. 'Doesn't' ist für die Verneinung im Present Simple.

items.p: Your friends stopped playing football. Describe it. Make it negative: They are playing football. → They _____ football.
items.h: Add 'not' after 'are'. The -ing form stays the same.
items.hd: Fuege 'not' nach 'are' ein. Die -ing Form bleibt gleich.
items.e: To make present continuous negative, add 'not' after the 'be' verb: aren't playing.
items.ed: Um das Present Continuous zu verneinen, fuege 'not' nach dem 'be'-Verb ein: aren't playing.

items.p: You smell something delicious. Ask about your dad. Make it a question: He is cooking dinner. → _____ he _____ dinner?
a: Is, cooking he Is, cooking dinner?
Is, cooking he Is, cooking dinner
Is, cooking he Is, cooking dinner
items.h: For questions, put 'is/are' before the subject.
items.hd: Für Fragen kommt 'is/are' vor das Subjekt.
items.e: To make a present continuous question, put 'be' before the subject: Is he cooking?
items.ed: Um eine Frage im Present Continuous zu bilden, kommt 'be' vor das Subjekt: Is he cooking?

items.p: Translate into English: Sie spielt gerade Tennis.
items.h: 'gerade' = right now → present continuous (am/is/are + verb-ing).
items.hd: 'gerade' zeigt an, dass es JETZT passiert → Present Continuous (am/is/are + Verb-ing).
items.e: German 'gerade' signals an ongoing action → use present continuous in English.
items.ed: Deutsch 'gerade' zeigt eine laufende Handlung an → im Englischen Present Continuous verwenden.

items.p: Translate into English: Schlaefst du gerade? – Nein.
items.c: Are you sleeping? – No, I'm not.
a: Are you sleeping? – No, I'm not.
Are you sleeping? - No, I'm not.
Are you sleeping? No, I'm not.
Are you sleeping? – No, I am not.
Are you sleeping? – No, I'm not
Are you sleeping? - No, I am not.
Are you sleeping? No, I am not.
items.h: Question: put 'are' before 'you'. Short answer: No, I'm not.
items.hd: Frage: stelle 'are' vor 'you'. Kurzantwort: No, I'm not.
items.e: Present continuous question: Are you sleeping? Negative short answer: No, I'm not. (Never 'No, I amn't.')
items.ed: Frage im Present Continuous: Are you sleeping? Verneinte Kurzantwort: No, I'm not. (Niemals 'No, I amn't.')

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: is / homework / doing / she / her
items.h: Start with the subject. Then the 'be' verb. Then the -ing verb.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt. Dann das 'be'-Verb. Dann das -ing-Verb.
items.e: Subject (She) + be (is) + verb-ing (doing) + object (her homework).
items.ed: Subjekt (She) + be (is) + Verb-ing (doing) + Objekt (her homework).

items.p: She is reading a book. → Ask a yes/no question.
items.h: Put 'is' before 'she' to make a question.
items.hd: Stelle 'is' vor 'she', um eine Frage zu bilden.
items.e: To form a present continuous question, put 'be' before the subject: Is she reading a book?
items.ed: Um eine Frage im Present Continuous zu bilden, stelle 'be' vor das Subjekt: Is she reading a book?

items.p: You come home and see your cat sleeping. Which sentence describes what is happening right now?
items.c: Look! The cat is sleeping on my bed.
a: Look! The cat is sleeping on my bed.
ds: Look! The cat sleeps on my bed.
Look! The cat slept on my bed.
Look! The cat sleep on my bed.
items.h: 'Look!' means something is happening right now. Use is/am/are + verb-ing.
items.hd: 'Look!' bedeutet, etwas passiert gerade jetzt. Verwende is/am/are + Verb-ing.
items.e: 'Look!' + is sleeping = present continuous. It describes what's happening at this moment.
items.ed: 'Look!' + is sleeping = Present Continuous. Es beschreibt, was gerade in diesem Moment passiert.

items.p: Look! The dog is ___ (run) in the park.
items.h: Short vowel + single consonant → double the consonant before -ing.
items.hd: Kurzer Vokal + einzelner Konsonant → verdopple den Konsonanten vor -ing.
items.e: run → running. Short vowel (u) + single consonant (n) = double the -n before -ing.
items.ed: run → running. Kurzer Vokal (u) + einzelner Konsonant (n) = verdopple das -n vor -ing.

items.p: What ___ you doing right now?
items.h: 'You' + which form of 'be'? And what's the -ing form of 'do'?
items.hd: 'You' + welche Form von 'be'? Und wie ist die -ing Form von 'do'?
items.e: What are you doing? 'You' → 'are'. 'do' → 'doing'.
items.ed: What are you doing? 'You' → 'are'. 'do' → 'doing'.

items.p: I _____ at home yesterday.
items.h: 'I' always goes with 'was' in the past.
items.hd: 'I' geht in der Vergangenheit immer mit 'was'.
items.e: I was, he/she/it was. You/we/they were. I was at home yesterday.
items.ed: I was, he/she/it was. You/we/they were. I was at home yesterday.

items.p: They _____ at school last Monday.
items.h: 'They' always goes with 'were' in the past.
items.hd: 'They' geht in der Vergangenheit immer mit 'were'.
items.e: You/we/they + were. They were at school last Monday.
items.ed: You/we/they + were. They were at school last Monday.

items.p: She _____ very tired after the party.
items.h: 'She' is 3rd person singular — which past form of 'be' do we use?
items.hd: 'She' ist 3. Person Einzahl — welche Vergangenheitsform von 'be' verwenden wir?
items.e: He/she/it + was. She was very tired.
items.ed: He/she/it + was. She was very tired.

items.p: We _____ in London last summer.
items.h: 'We' goes with 'were' in the past.
items.hd: 'We' geht in der Vergangenheit mit 'were'.
items.e: We were in London. 'We' always takes 'were' in past simple.
items.ed: We were in London. 'We' nimmt in der einfachen Vergangenheit immer 'were'.

items.p: The film _____ really good!
items.h: 'The film' is singular. Past tense of 'be' for singular = ?
items.hd: 'The film' ist Einzahl. Vergangenheitsform von 'be' für Einzahl = ?
items.e: 'The film' is 3rd person singular → was. The film was really good!
items.ed: 'The film' ist 3. Person Einzahl → was. The film was really good!

items.p: She _____ happy because her cat _____ ill.
items.h: 'She' → was/wasn't. 'Her cat' → was/wasn't. One is negative.
items.hd: 'She' → was/wasn't. 'Her cat' → was/wasn't. Eines ist verneint.
items.e: She wasn't happy (negative) because her cat was ill (affirmative). Both use 'was' (3rd person singular).
items.ed: She wasn't happy (Verneinung) because her cat was ill (Bejahung). Beide verwenden 'was' (3. Person Einzahl).

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.h: 'They' is plural → which past form of 'be'?
items.hd: 'They' ist Mehrzahl → welche Vergangenheitsform von 'be'?
items.e: They + were. 'Was' is for I/he/she/it. 'Yesterday' requires past tense.
items.ed: They + were. 'Was' ist für I/he/she/it. 'Yesterday' erfordert die Vergangenheit.

items.p: Choose the correct short answer: 'Were you at the party?' – 'No, _____.'
items.h: 'I' in the past uses 'was/wasn't'. The answer is 'No', so it's negative.
items.hd: 'I' in der Vergangenheit verwendet 'was/wasn't'. Die Antwort ist 'No', also verneint.
items.e: No, I wasn't. Short answers use the same 'be' form as the question, just negative: wasn't.
items.ed: No, I wasn't. Kurzantworten verwenden dieselbe 'be'-Form wie die Frage, nur verneint: wasn't.

items.p: Choose the correct question: '_____ she at school yesterday?'
items.h: Think about whether the subject is singular or plural, then choose the right past form.
items.hd: Ist das Subjekt Einzahl oder Mehrzahl? Wähle die richtige Vergangenheitsform.
items.e: 'She' is singular → Was she at school? 'Were' is for you/we/they.
items.ed: 'She' ist Einzahl → Was she at school? 'Were' ist für you/we/they.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: They was at the zoo yesterday.
items.c: They were at the zoo yesterday.
a: They were at the zoo yesterday.
items.h: 'They' is plural. Do we use 'was' or 'were' with plural subjects?
items.hd: 'They' ist Mehrzahl. Verwenden wir 'was' oder 'were' mit Mehrzahl-Subjekten?
items.e: 'They' is plural → were, not was. They were at the zoo yesterday.
items.ed: 'They' ist Mehrzahl → were, nicht was. They were at the zoo yesterday.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I were at the cinema last night.
items.c: I was at the cinema last night.
a: I was at the cinema last night.
items.h: 'I' always goes with 'was', not 'were'.
items.hd: 'I' geht immer mit 'was', nicht 'were'.
items.e: 'I' + was (not were). I was at the cinema last night.
items.ed: 'I' + was (nicht were). I was at the cinema last night.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I was been tired yesterday.
items.h: Is 'been' needed here? What is the simple past of 'be'?
items.hd: Brauchen wir hier 'been'? Wie lautet die einfache Vergangenheit von 'be'?
items.e: Past simple of 'be' = was/were. No 'been' needed — that's the past participle (used in present perfect).
items.ed: Past Simple von 'be' = was/were. Kein 'been' nötig — das ist das Past Participle (wird im Present Perfect verwendet).

items.p: Today you are happy, but yesterday was different. Change from present to past: I am happy. → Yesterday I _____ happy.
items.h: am/is → was in the past. are → were in the past.
items.hd: am/is → was in der Vergangenheit. are → were in der Vergangenheit.
items.e: 'I am' → 'I was' in the past. Yesterday I was happy.
items.ed: 'I am' → 'I was' in der Vergangenheit. Yesterday I was happy.

items.p: Your mum wants to know where your brother was. Help her ask. Make it a question: He was at home. → _____ he at home?
items.h: For questions with 'be', put the verb before the subject.
items.hd: Für Fragen mit 'be' kommt das Verb vor das Subjekt.
items.e: He was at home. → Was he at home? (invert subject and verb for questions)
items.ed: He was at home. → Was he at home? (Subjekt und Verb tauschen für Fragen)

items.p: Translate into English: Wir waren gestern im Kino.
items.c: We were at the cinema yesterday.
a: We were at the cinema yesterday.
We were at the cinema yesterday
Yesterday we were at the cinema.
We were at the movies yesterday.
Yesterday we were at the movies.
items.h: 'Wir' = we. 'waren' = were. 'Kino' = cinema.
items.hd: 'Wir' = we. 'waren' = were. 'Kino' = cinema.
items.e: We were at the cinema. 'We' takes 'were' in the past.
items.ed: We were at the cinema. 'We' nimmt 'were' in der Vergangenheit.

items.p: Translate into English: Er war gestern nicht in der Schule.
a: He was not at school yesterday.
Yesterday he was not at school.
items.h: 'nicht' = not. 'was not' = wasn't.
items.hd: 'nicht' = not. 'was not' = wasn't.
items.e: He wasn't (was not) at school yesterday. 'He' → 'was' → negative: 'wasn't'.
items.ed: He wasn't (was not) at school yesterday. 'He' → 'was' → Verneinung: 'wasn't'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: at / were / yesterday / the park / we
items.c: We were at the park yesterday.
a: We were at the park yesterday.
Yesterday we were at the park.
items.h: Start with the subject (We). Then the verb (were).
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt (We). Dann das Verb (were).
items.e: Subject + verb + place + time: We were at the park yesterday.
items.ed: Subjekt + Verb + Ort + Zeit: We were at the park yesterday.

items.p: You are talking about last weekend. Which sentence about the past is correct?
items.c: We were at the zoo yesterday.
a: We were at the zoo yesterday.
ds: We was at the zoo yesterday.
We are at the zoo yesterday.
We be at the zoo yesterday.
items.h: I/he/she/it → was. You/we/they → were.
items.hd: I/he/she/it → was. You/we/they → were.
items.e: 'Yesterday' = past. We + were (not 'was'). We were at the zoo.
items.ed: 'Yesterday' = Vergangenheit. We + were (nicht 'was'). We were at the zoo.

items.p: ___ you at the birthday party last Saturday?
items.h: 'You' always goes with 'were' in the past.
items.hd: 'You' geht in der Vergangenheit immer mit 'were'.
items.e: Were you at the party? 'You' + 'were' in past questions.
items.ed: Were you at the party? 'You' + 'were' in Fragen in der Vergangenheit.

items.p: The children ___ (not / be) at home. They were at school.
items.h: 'Children' is plural → were/weren't. First sentence is negative.
items.hd: 'Children' ist Mehrzahl → were/weren't. Der erste Satz ist verneint.
items.e: The children weren't at home. They were at the park. 'Children' = plural → were/weren't.
items.ed: The children weren't at home. They were at the park. 'Children' = Mehrzahl → were/weren't.

items.p: January is the _____ month of the year.
items.h: 1 → first. This is an exception — it doesn't end in -th!
items.hd: 1 → first. Das ist eine Ausnahme — es endet nicht auf -th!
items.e: 'First' is the ordinal form of 'one'. It's irregular — you must learn it by heart.
items.ed: 'First' ist die Ordnungszahl von 'one'. Sie ist unregelmäßig — du musst sie auswendig lernen.

items.p: Wednesday is the _____ day of the school week.
items.h: 3 → third. Another exception!
items.hd: 3 → third. Noch eine Ausnahme!
items.e: 'Third' is the ordinal form of 'three'. It's irregular.
items.ed: 'Third' ist die Ordnungszahl von 'three'. Sie ist unregelmäßig.

items.p: She came _____ in the race. (5th place)
items.h: five → fifth. The -ve changes to -f before -th.
items.hd: five → fifth. Das -ve wird zu -f vor -th.
items.e: five → fifth (not fiveth!). The spelling changes: -ve → -f + -th.
items.ed: five → fifth (nicht fiveth!). Die Schreibweise ändert sich: -ve → -f + -th.

items.p: Today is the _____ of March. (9)
items.h: nine → ninth. Drop the -e before adding -th.
items.hd: nine → ninth. Das -e faellt weg vor -th.
items.e: nine → ninth (drop the -e). Spelling change: nineth is wrong!
items.ed: nine → ninth (das -e faellt weg). Rechtschreibaenderung: nineth ist falsch!

items.p: Christmas is on the twenty-_____ of December.
items.h: 25th = twenty-fifth. How do you spell the ordinal of 'five'?
items.hd: 25th = twenty-fifth. Wie buchstabiert man die Ordnungszahl von 'five'?
items.e: twenty-fifth (25th). Five → fifth (spelling change: -ve → -f + -th).
items.ed: twenty-fifth (25th). Five → fifth (Rechtschreibaenderung: -ve → -f + -th).

items.p: Which is the correct ordinal form of 12?
items.h: Some numbers change their spelling when they become ordinals. Think about how the ending changes.
items.hd: Manche Zahlen ändern ihre Schreibweise als Ordnungszahlen.
items.e: twelve → twelfth. Like 'five → fifth', the -ve changes to -f before -th.
items.ed: twelve → twelfth. Wie bei 'five → fifth' ändert sich -ve zu -f vor -th.

items.p: Which ordinal number is correct?
items.h: Think about how you add -th to this number. Does the spelling stay the same?
items.hd: Überlege, wie du -th an diese Zahl anhängst.
items.e: eight → eighth. Since 'eight' ends in -t, you just add -h: eighth.
items.ed: eight → eighth. Da 'eight' auf -t endet, fuegst du einfach -h hinzu: eighth.

items.p: Which is the correct way to write 2nd as a word?
items.h: This ordinal is completely irregular — it doesn't follow the normal -th pattern.
items.hd: Diese Ordnungszahl ist völlig unregelmäßig.
items.e: 'Second' is the ordinal form of 'two'. It's completely irregular — no -th ending!
items.ed: 'Second' ist die Ordnungszahl von 'two'. Sie ist völlig unregelmäßig — keine -th-Endung!

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: My birthday is on the nineth of June.
items.c: My birthday is on the ninth of June.
a: My birthday is on the ninth of June.
items.h: nine → ninth. Does the -e stay or go?
items.hd: nine → ninth. Bleibt das -e oder faellt es weg?
items.e: nine → ninth (drop the -e). 'Nineth' is a common spelling mistake.
items.ed: nine → ninth (das -e faellt weg). 'Nineth' ist ein häufiger Rechtschreibfehler.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She was fiveth in the race.
items.c: She was fifth in the race.
a: She was fifth in the race.
items.h: five → fifth. The -ve changes to -f.
items.hd: five → fifth. Das -ve wird zu -f.
items.e: five → fifth (not fiveth). The -ve changes to -f before -th.
items.ed: five → fifth (nicht fiveth). Das -ve wird zu -f vor -th.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: December is the twelveth month.
items.h: twelve → twelfth. Like 'five → fifth', the -ve changes.
items.hd: twelve → twelfth. Wie bei 'five → fifth' ändert sich das -ve.
items.e: twelve → twelfth (not twelveth). The -ve changes to -f before -th.
items.ed: twelve → twelfth (nicht twelveth). Das -ve wird zu -f vor -th.

items.p: Write the ordinal number as a word: 20th → _____
items.h: twenty → twentieth. The -y changes to -ie before -th.
items.hd: twenty → twentieth. Das -y wird zu -ie vor -th.
items.e: twenty → twentieth. Numbers ending in -y change to -ie before -th.
items.ed: twenty → twentieth. Zahlen auf -y werden zu -ie vor -th.

items.p: It's the last day of the month and you need to write it on the board. Write the ordinal number as a word: 31st → _____
items.h: 31st = thirty + first. Compounds use a hyphen.
items.hd: 31st = thirty + first. Zusammengesetzte Zahlen verwenden einen Bindestrich.
items.e: 31st = thirty-first. The 'tens' part stays regular, the 'ones' part follows ordinal rules.
items.ed: 31st = thirty-first. Der Zehnerteil bleibt regelmäßig, der Einerteil folgt den Ordnungszahlregeln.

items.p: Translate into English: Mein Geburtstag ist am dritten Mai.
items.c: My birthday is on the third of May.
a: My birthday is on the third of May.
My birthday is on the 3rd of May.
items.h: 'dritten' = third. Dates use 'on the ... of ...'
items.hd: 'dritten' = third. Datumsangaben verwenden 'on the ... of ...'
items.e: German 'am dritten Mai' = 'on the third of May'. English dates use ordinal numbers.
items.ed: Deutsch 'am dritten Mai' = 'on the third of May'. Englische Datumsangaben verwenden Ordnungszahlen.

items.p: Translate into English: Heute ist der fuenfzehnte Dezember.
items.c: Today is the fifteenth of December.
a: Today is the fifteenth of December.
Is the fifteenth of december today
items.h: 'fuenfzehnte' = fifteenth. fifteen + th = fifteenth.
items.hd: 'fünfzehnte' = fifteenth. fifteen + th = fifteenth.
items.e: fifteen → fifteenth (regular: just add -th). Today is the fifteenth of December.
items.ed: fifteen → fifteenth (regelmäßig: einfach -th anhängen). Today is the fifteenth of December.

items.p: Match the cardinal number with its ordinal form:
ds: a: third|b: ninth|c: twelfth|d: first|e: fifth
items.h: Focus on the irregular ones: 1st, 3rd, 5th, 9th, 12th.
items.hd: Konzentriere dich auf die unregelmäßigen: 1st, 3rd, 5th, 9th, 12th.
items.e: one→first, three→third, five→fifth, nine→ninth, twelve→twelfth.
items.ed: one→first, three→third, five→fifth, nine→ninth, twelve→twelfth.

items.p: Today is the ___ of June. (21)
items.h: 21st = twenty-first. 06 = June.
items.hd: 21st = twenty-first. 06 = June.
items.e: 21st = twenty-first. June is the sixth month. Today is the twenty-first of June.
items.ed: 21st = twenty-first. June ist der sechste Monat. Today is the twenty-first of June.

items.p: December has 31 days. The last day is the ___.
items.h: thirty → thirtieth. The -y changes to -ie before -th.
items.hd: thirty → thirtieth. Das -y wird zu -ie vor -th.
items.e: thirty → thirtieth. Like twentieth, the -y changes to -ie before -th.
items.ed: thirty → thirtieth. Wie bei twentieth ändert sich -y zu -ie vor -th.

items.p: I have English _____ Monday.
items.h: We use a specific preposition with days of the week.
items.hd: Wir verwenden eine bestimmte Präposition mit Wochentagen.
items.e: ON + days: on Monday, on Tuesday, on Friday. Not 'in' or 'at'!
items.ed: ON + Tage: on Monday, on Tuesday, on Friday. Nicht 'in' oder 'at'!

items.p: My birthday is _____ March.
items.h: Which preposition do we use with months?
items.hd: Welche Präposition verwenden wir mit Monaten?
items.e: IN + months: in March, in July, in December.
items.ed: IN + Monate: in March, in July, in December.

items.p: School starts _____ 8 o'clock.
items.h: Which preposition do we use with clock times?
items.hd: Welche Präposition verwenden wir mit Uhrzeiten?
items.e: AT + clock times: at 8 o'clock, at half past three, at noon.
items.ed: AT + Uhrzeiten: at 8 o'clock, at half past three, at noon.

items.p: We go skiing _____ winter.
items.h: Seasons are like months — they use the same preposition.
items.hd: Jahreszeiten verwenden dieselbe Präposition wie Monate.
items.e: IN + seasons: in winter, in summer, in spring, in autumn.
items.ed: IN + Jahreszeiten: in winter, in summer, in spring, in autumn.

items.p: Her birthday is _____ the 15th of May.
items.h: We use 'on' with days AND dates.
items.hd: Wir verwenden 'on' mit Tagen UND Datumsangaben.
items.e: ON + dates: on the 15th of May, on December 25th, on my birthday.
items.ed: ON + Datumsangaben: on the 15th of May, on December 25th, on my birthday.

items.p: I wake up early _____ the morning.
items.h: We say 'in the morning', 'in the afternoon', 'in the evening' — but 'at night'!
items.hd: Wir sagen 'in the morning', 'in the afternoon', 'in the evening' — aber 'at night'!
items.e: IN the morning/afternoon/evening. But AT night (exception!).
items.ed: IN the morning/afternoon/evening. Aber AT night (Ausnahme!).

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: The concert is on Saturday at 7 o'clock.
a: The concert is on Saturday at 7 o'clock.
ds: The concert is in Saturday at 7 o'clock.
The concert is at Saturday on 7 o'clock.
The concert is on Saturday in 7 o'clock.
items.h: Days → on. Clock times → at.
items.hd: Tage → on. Uhrzeiten → at.
items.e: ON Saturday (day) + AT 7 o'clock (time). The concert is on Saturday at 7 o'clock.
items.ed: ON Saturday (Tag) + AT 7 o'clock (Uhrzeit). The concert is on Saturday at 7 o'clock.

items.p: Choose the correct preposition: 'We have a test _____ Friday.'
items.h: Think about what type of time word this is — a day, a month, or a time of day?
items.hd: Um welche Art von Zeitangabe handelt es sich — Tag, Monat oder Uhrzeit?
items.e: ON + days: on Friday, on Monday, on Wednesday.
items.ed: ON + Tage: on Friday, on Monday, on Wednesday.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I have sports in Monday.
items.h: Which preposition do we use with days? 'In', 'on', or 'at'?
items.hd: Welche Präposition verwenden wir mit Tagen? 'In', 'on' oder 'at'?
items.e: Days → ON. 'I have sports on Monday.' German 'am Montag' becomes 'on Monday' in English.
items.ed: Tage → ON. 'I have sports on Monday.' Deutsch 'am Montag' wird zu 'on Monday' im Englischen.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: My birthday is on December.
items.h: Months use a different preposition than days.
items.hd: Monate verwenden eine andere Präposition als Tage.
items.e: Months → IN. 'My birthday is in December.' 'On' is for days and dates, not months alone.
items.ed: Monate → IN. 'My birthday is in December.' 'On' ist für Tage und Datumsangaben, nicht für Monate allein.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I was born in 15th March.
a: I was born on the 15th of March.
items.h: '15th March' is a specific date. Dates use which preposition?
items.hd: '15th March' ist ein bestimmtes Datum. Datumsangaben verwenden welche Präposition?
items.e: Dates → ON. 'I was born on 15th March.' Even though months use 'in', specific dates use 'on'.
items.ed: Datumsangaben → ON. 'I was born on 15th March.' Auch wenn Monate 'in' verwenden, verwenden konkrete Datumsangaben 'on'.

items.p: Add the correct preposition: The party is _____ Saturday _____ the afternoon _____ 3 o'clock.
a: Add the correct preposition: The party is on, in, at Saturday on, in, at the afternoon on, in, at 3 o'clock.
Add the correct preposition: The party is on, in, at Saturday on, in, at the afternoon on, in, at 3 o'clock
Add the correct preposition: The party is on, in, at Saturday on, in, at the afternoon on, in, at 3 o'clock
items.h: Day → on. Time of day → in. Clock time → at.
items.hd: Tag → on. Tageszeit → in. Uhrzeit → at.
items.e: ON Saturday (day) + IN the afternoon (time of day) + AT 3 o'clock (clock time).
items.ed: ON Saturday (Tag) + IN the afternoon (Tageszeit) + AT 3 o'clock (Uhrzeit).

items.p: Fill in: I go swimming _____ Thursdays _____ summer.
a: Fill in: I go swimming on, in Thursdays on, in summer.
Fill in: I go swimming on, in Thursdays on, in summer
Fill in: I go swimming on, in Thursdays on, in summer
items.h: Days → on. Seasons → in.
items.hd: Tage → on. Jahreszeiten → in.
items.e: ON Thursdays (day) + IN summer (season). I go swimming on Thursdays in summer.
items.ed: ON Thursdays (Tag) + IN summer (Jahreszeit). I go swimming on Thursdays in summer.

items.p: Translate into English: Ich habe am Freitag einen Test.
items.c: I have a test on Friday.
a: I have a test on Friday.
I have a test on Friday
I've got a test on Friday.
I have got a test on Friday.
items.h: 'Am Freitag' = on Friday. Days → on.
items.hd: 'Am Freitag' = on Friday. Tage → on.
items.e: German 'am Freitag' = 'on Friday'. Days always use 'on' in English.
items.ed: Deutsch 'am Freitag' = 'on Friday'. Tage verwenden im Englischen immer 'on'.

items.p: Translate into English: Die Schule beginnt um 8 Uhr im September.
items.c: School starts at 8 o'clock in September.
a: School starts at 8 o'clock in September.
items.h: 'um 8 Uhr' = at 8 o'clock. 'im September' = in September.
items.hd: 'um 8 Uhr' = at 8 o'clock. 'im September' = in September.
items.e: AT 8 o'clock (time) + IN September (month). School starts at 8 o'clock in September.
items.ed: AT 8 o'clock (Uhrzeit) + IN September (Monat). School starts at 8 o'clock in September.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: on / party / the / Saturday / is
items.h: Start with 'The party'. Then the verb. Then the time expression.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'The party'. Dann das Verb. Dann der Zeitausdruck.
items.e: The party (subject) + is (verb) + on Saturday (time with correct preposition).
items.ed: The party (Subjekt) + is (Verb) + on Saturday (Zeit mit richtiger Präposition).

items.p: Match the time expression with the correct preposition:
ds: 1: Monday|2: 8 o'clock|3: summer|4: my birthday|5: night|6: January
items.h: ON = days/dates. IN = months/seasons/morning. AT = clock times/night.
items.hd: ON = Tage/Daten. IN = Monate/Jahreszeiten/Morgen. AT = Uhrzeiten/Nacht.
items.e: ON Monday, AT 8 o'clock, IN summer, ON my birthday, AT night, IN January.
items.ed: ON Monday, AT 8 o'clock, IN summer, ON my birthday, AT night, IN January.

items.p: I play football ___ Saturdays.
items.h: Days → on. Time of day → in.
items.hd: Tage → on. Tageszeit → in.
items.e: ON Saturdays (day) + IN the afternoon (time of day).
items.ed: ON Saturdays (Tag) + IN the afternoon (Tageszeit).

items.p: My birthday is ___ the 21st of March.
items.h: Dates → on. Time of day → in. Clock time → at.
items.hd: Datumsangaben → on. Tageszeit → in. Uhrzeit → at.
items.e: ON the 21st (date) + IN the evening (time of day) + AT 6 o'clock (clock time).
items.ed: ON the 21st (Datum) + IN the evening (Tageszeit) + AT 6 o'clock (Uhrzeit).

items.p: Yesterday she _____ (walk) to school.
items.h: For the past tense of regular verbs, add -ed.
items.hd: Für die Vergangenheit regelmäßiger Verben hänge -ed an.
items.e: walk + -ed = walked. Regular verbs form the past by adding -ed.
items.ed: walk + -ed = walked. regelmäßige Verben bilden die Vergangenheit mit -ed.

items.p: We _____ (play) football after school.
items.h: play + -ed. Careful: 'play' ends in vowel + y, so just add -ed!
items.hd: play + -ed. Achtung: 'play' endet auf Vokal + y, also einfach -ed anhängen!
items.e: play → played (vowel + y: just add -ed). Only consonant + y changes to -ied.
items.ed: play → played (Vokal + y: einfach -ed anhängen). Nur Konsonant + y wird zu -ied.

items.p: She _____ (like) the film very much.
items.h: If the verb already ends in -e, just add -d (not -ed).
items.hd: Wenn das Verb bereits auf -e endet, hänge nur -d an (nicht -ed).
items.e: like → liked. Verbs ending in -e just add -d: like + d = liked.
items.ed: like → liked. Verben auf -e bekommen nur -d: like + d = liked.

items.p: He _____ (stop) the car quickly.
items.h: Short vowel + single consonant → double the consonant before -ed.
items.hd: Kurzer Vokal + einzelner Konsonant → Konsonant verdoppeln vor -ed.
items.e: stop → stopped. Short vowel (o) + single consonant (p) = double p + -ed.
items.ed: stop → stopped. Kurzer Vokal (o) + einzelner Konsonant (p) = pp + -ed.

items.p: They _____ (carry) the bags home.
items.h: Consonant + y → change y to i and add -ed.
items.hd: Konsonant + y → y wird zu i und -ed wird angehaengt.
items.e: carry → carried. Consonant + y: change y to i, then add -ed.
items.ed: carry → carried. Konsonant + y: y wird zu i, dann -ed anhängen.

items.p: The hikers _____ (slip) on the ice and _____ (shout) for help.
items.h: slip → slipped (double p). shout → shouted (no doubling needed).
items.hd: slip → slipped (p verdoppeln). shout → shouted (keine Verdopplung nötig).
items.e: slip → slipped (short vowel + consonant = double). shout → shouted (two vowels before consonant = no double).
items.ed: slip → slipped (kurzer Vokal + Konsonant = Verdopplung). shout → shouted (zwei Vokale vor Konsonant = keine Verdopplung).

items.p: Which past tense form is spelled correctly?
items.h: stop → stopp... (double the p) + -ed.
items.hd: stop → stopp... (p verdoppeln) + -ed.
items.e: stop → stopped. Double the consonant after a short vowel, then add -ed.
items.ed: stop → stopped. Nach einem kurzen Vokal den Konsonanten verdoppeln, dann -ed anhängen.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
ds: Yesterday I was walked to school.
items.h: 'Yesterday' signals past. Walk is regular → add -ed.
items.hd: 'Yesterday' zeigt Vergangenheit. Walk ist regelmäßig → -ed anhängen.
items.e: walk → walked. Just add -ed. 'Go' is irregular (went), but 'walk' is regular.
items.ed: walk → walked. Einfach -ed anhängen. 'Go' ist unregelmäßig (went), aber 'walk' ist regelmäßig.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He stoped the car.
items.h: stop has a short vowel + single consonant. What happens before -ed?
items.hd: stop hat einen kurzen Vokal + einzelnen Konsonanten. Was passiert vor -ed?
items.e: stop → stopped (double the p). Short vowel + single consonant = double before -ed.
items.ed: stop → stopped (p verdoppeln). Kurzer Vokal + einzelner Konsonant = verdoppeln vor -ed.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She cryed when her cat ran away.
items.c: She cried when her cat ran away.
a: She cried when her cat ran away.
items.h: cry → cri... (y becomes i) + -ed. Not 'cryed'.
items.hd: cry → cri... (y wird zu i) + -ed. Nicht 'cryed'.
items.e: cry → cried (consonant + y: change y to i, add -ed). 'Cryed' is wrong.
items.ed: cry → cried (Konsonant + y: y wird zu i, -ed anhängen). 'Cryed' ist falsch.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Yesterday I watch a great film.
items.c: Yesterday I watched a great film.
a: Yesterday I watched a great film.
items.h: 'Yesterday' = past. Add -ed to the verb.
items.hd: 'Yesterday' = Vergangenheit. Hänge -ed an das Verb.
items.e: With 'yesterday', we need past tense: watch → watched.
items.ed: Mit 'yesterday' brauchen wir die Vergangenheit: watch → watched.

items.p: Write the past simple form: arrive → _____
items.h: arrive ends in -e. Just add -d.
items.hd: arrive endet auf -e. Hänge einfach -d an.
items.e: arrive → arrived (ends in -e, just add -d).
items.ed: arrive → arrived (endet auf -e, einfach -d anhängen).

items.p: You planned a surprise party for your mum last week. How do you write it? Write the past simple form: plan → _____
items.h: Short vowel (a) + single consonant (n) → double the consonant.
items.hd: Kurzer Vokal (a) + einzelner Konsonant (n) → Konsonant verdoppeln.
items.e: plan → planned. Short vowel + single consonant = double n + -ed.
items.ed: plan → planned. Kurzer Vokal + einzelner Konsonant = nn + -ed.

items.p: Translate into English: Gestern hat sie ihre Oma besucht.
items.h: 'besucht' = visited. visit → visited (regular -ed).
items.hd: 'besucht' = visited. visit → visited (regelmäßig -ed).
items.e: visit → visited. 'Yesterday' signals past tense.
items.ed: visit → visited. 'Yesterday' (gestern) zeigt die Vergangenheit an.

items.p: Translate into English: Wir haben letztes Wochenende im Park Fussball gespielt.
items.c: We played football in the park last weekend.
a: We played football in the park last weekend.
Last weekend we played football in the park.
End we played football in the park last week
items.h: 'gespielt' = played. 'letztes Wochenende' = last weekend.
items.hd: 'gespielt' = played. 'letztes Wochenende' = last weekend.
items.e: play → played (vowel + y, just add -ed). English uses past simple, not 'have played' here.
items.ed: play → played (Vokal + y, einfach -ed). Im Englischen steht hier Past Simple, nicht 'have played'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: yesterday / helped / I / mum / my
items.h: Subject first, then the verb in past tense.
items.hd: Subjekt zuerst, dann das Verb in der Vergangenheit.
items.e: I helped my mum yesterday. (Subject + past verb + object + time)
items.ed: I helped my mum yesterday. (Subjekt + Verb in Vergangenheit + Objekt + Zeit)

items.p: Last night we ___ (watch) a film with our family.
items.h: Both verbs are regular. Just add -ed.
items.hd: Beide Verben sind regelmäßig. Einfach -ed anhängen.
items.e: watch → watched, listen → listened. Both regular — add -ed.
items.ed: watch → watched, listen → listened. Beide regelmäßig — -ed anhängen.

items.p: She ___ (study) hard for the test last week.
items.h: Both end in consonant + y. Remember the spelling rule!
items.hd: Beide enden auf Konsonant + y. Denke an die Rechtschreibregel!
items.e: study → studied, try → tried. Consonant + y → change y to i, add -ed.
items.ed: study → studied, try → tried. Konsonant + y → y wird zu i, -ed anhängen.

items.p: I was hungry _____ I ate a sandwich.
items.h: The two actions go together. She was hungry, then she ate.
items.hd: Die beiden Handlungen gehören zusammen. Sie war hungrig, dann hat sie gegessen.
items.e: 'And' connects two actions that go together: I was hungry and I ate.
items.ed: 'And' verbindet zwei Handlungen, die zusammengehören: I was hungry and I ate.

items.p: She was tired _____ she didn't stop running.
items.h: You'd expect a tired person to stop. She didn't — that's surprising.
items.hd: Man würde erwarten, dass eine muede Person aufhoert. Sie hat es nicht getan — das ist überraschend.
items.e: 'But' shows contrast/surprise: she was tired BUT she didn't stop.
items.ed: 'But' zeigt einen Gegensatz/eine Überraschung: she was tired BUT she didn't stop.

items.p: He was scared _____ it was dark.
items.h: Why was he scared? The darkness is the REASON.
items.hd: Warum hatte er Angst? Die Dunkelheit ist der GRUND.
items.e: 'Because' gives a reason: He was scared BECAUSE it was dark.
items.ed: 'Because' gibt einen Grund an: He was scared BECAUSE it was dark.

items.p: We wanted to go to the park _____ it was raining.
items.h: Going to the park + rain = two things that don't go well together.
items.hd: In den Park gehen + Regen = zwei Dinge, die nicht gut zusammenpassen.
items.e: 'But' shows contrast: wanting to go to the park DESPITE the rain.
items.ed: 'But' zeigt einen Gegensatz: in den Park gehen wollen TROTZ des Regens.

items.p: She didn't go to school _____ she was ill.
items.h: Why didn't she go? Being ill is the reason.
items.hd: Warum ist sie nicht gegangen? Krank sein ist der Grund.
items.e: 'Because' gives the reason: She didn't go to school because she was ill.
items.ed: 'Because' gibt den Grund an: She didn't go to school because she was ill.

items.p: Choose the correct linking word: 'I like cats _____ I don't like dogs.'
items.h: Liking one thing and NOT liking another = contrast.
items.hd: Etwas moegen und etwas anderes NICHT moegen = Gegensatz.
items.e: 'But' shows contrast: I like X BUT I don't like Y.
items.ed: 'But' zeigt einen Gegensatz: I like X BUT I don't like Y.

items.p: Which sentence uses 'because' correctly?
items.c: I stayed at home because I was tired.
a: I stayed at home because I was tired.
ds: Because I stayed at home I was tired.
I stayed at home because tired.
I because stayed at home was tired.
items.h: 'Because' introduces the REASON. The reason comes after 'because'.
items.hd: 'Because' leitet den GRUND ein. Der Grund kommt nach 'because'.
items.e: 'Because' + full clause (subject + verb): I stayed home because I was tired.
items.ed: 'Because' + vollstaendiger Satz (Subjekt + Verb): I stayed home because I was tired.

items.p: Choose the correct linking word: 'She slipped _____ fell down.'
items.h: Two things happened one after the other. No surprise, no reason.
items.hd: Zwei Dinge passierten nacheinander. Keine Überraschung, kein Grund.
items.e: 'And' connects two actions in sequence: She slipped AND fell down.
items.ed: 'And' verbindet zwei Handlungen in Reihenfolge: She slipped AND fell down.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I like pizza because is delicious.
items.c: I like pizza because it is delicious.
a: I like pizza because it is delicious.
I like pizza because it's delicious.
items.h: After 'because', you need a full sentence with a subject.
items.hd: Nach 'because' braucht man einen vollstaendigen Satz mit Subjekt.
items.e: 'Because' needs a full clause: because IT is delicious. Don't forget the subject!
items.ed: 'Because' braucht einen vollstaendigen Nebensatz: because IT is delicious. Vergiss das Subjekt nicht!

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She studied hard because she failed the test.
items.c: She studied hard but she failed the test.
a: She studied hard but she failed the test.
She studied hard but failed the test.
items.h: Does the second part show a reason or a surprise/contrast?
items.hd: Zeigt der zweite Teil einen Grund oder einen Gegensatz?
items.e: Use 'but' for contrast: He shouted BUT nobody heard him (unexpected result).
items.ed: Verwende 'but' für Gegensätze: He shouted BUT nobody heard him (unerwartetes Ergebnis).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I was tired but I didn't sleep well.
items.c: I was tired because I didn't sleep well.
a: I was tired because I didn't sleep well.
I was tired because I did not sleep well.
items.h: Does the second part explain the reason for being tired?
items.hd: Erklärt der zweite Teil den Grund für die Müdigkeit?
items.e: The bus being late is the REASON → use 'because', not 'but'.
items.ed: Der spaete Bus ist der GRUND → verwende 'because', nicht 'but'.

items.p: Translate into English: Er war muede, aber er spielte weiter.
items.c: He was tired but he kept playing.
a: He was tired but he kept playing.
He was tired, but he kept playing.
He was tired but he continued playing.
items.h: 'aber' = but (contrast). 'weiter spielen' = kept playing.
items.hd: 'aber' = but (Gegensatz). 'weiter spielen' = kept playing.
items.e: German 'aber' = English 'but'. Both show contrast.
items.ed: Deutsch 'aber' = Englisch 'but'. Beide zeigen einen Gegensatz.

items.p: I wanted an ice cream ___ the shop was closed.
items.h: Wanting ice cream + shop closed = contrast. Being sad + going home = addition/sequence.
items.hd: Eis wollen + Laden zu = Gegensatz. Traurig sein + nach Hause gehen = Ergaenzung/Reihenfolge.
items.e: I wanted ice cream BUT the shop was closed (contrast). I was sad AND went home (sequence).
items.ed: I wanted ice cream BUT the shop was closed (Gegensatz). I was sad AND went home (Reihenfolge).

items.p: We stayed inside ___ it was raining.
items.h: The rain is the REASON we stayed inside.
items.hd: Der Regen ist der GRUND, warum wir drinnen geblieben sind.
items.e: We stayed inside BECAUSE it was raining. Rain = reason for staying inside.
items.ed: We stayed inside BECAUSE it was raining. Regen = Grund für das Drinnenbleiben.

items.p: She _____ (not/play) football last weekend.
items.h: After 'didn't', use the BASE form of the verb (no -ed!).
items.hd: Nach 'didn't' verwende die GRUNDFORM des Verbs (kein -ed!).
items.e: didn't + play (base form). NOT 'didn't played'. 'Didn't' carries the past meaning.
items.ed: didn't + play (Grundform). NICHT 'didn't played'. 'Didn't' traegt die Vergangenheitsbedeutung.

items.p: They _____ (not/go) to the party.
items.h: Even with irregular verbs, use the BASE form after 'didn't': didn't GO (not went!).
